source.description:
Optional source descriptor.

When set, the config compiler resolves the listed source(s) before parsing the rest of the file. The remaining fields on `ConfigFile` are still honored: the file the source resolves to is itself a `ConfigFile`. When unset (the historical default), the file is treated as inline config.

proxy.description: Server-wide settings parsed from the top-level `proxy:` block.

origins.description: Map of hostname to per-origin configuration.

access_log.description: Optional structured-JSON access-log emission, off by default. When enabled, every completed request emits one JSON line via the tracing `access_log` target. See [`AccessLogConfig`] for filtering and sampling controls.

agent_classes.description: Top-level agent-class catalog selection and resolver tuning. When unset, the binary constructs a resolver from the embedded default catalog (so per-agent metric labels keep firing); operators only set this block when they want to point at a hosted feed, merge a custom catalog, or change the rDNS / bot-auth / cache settings.

rate_limits.description: WOR-1130: top-level workspace rate-limit budget + auto-suspend escalation (the R2.3 / A2.5 contract). Distinct from the per-origin `rate_limits` policy: this is a workspace-wide ceiling with a soft / throttle / auto-suspend state machine.

audit.description: WOR-1130: audit sink selection for admin-action audit rows (e.g. the auto-suspend transition). `memory` keeps the last N rows queryable via `/api/audit/recent` (used by tests + ops).

session_ledger.description: WOR-1186: emit the canonical session ledger (per-tool-call run records) from the live MCP `tools/call` path. Off unless this block is present and `enabled: true`.

update.description: WOR-1804: how `sbproxy update` behaves for the binary and the managed inference engines. Optional; an absent block is the same as the defaults (stable channel, no background check).

ConfigSource.description:
Where to load a `sb.yml` config text from.

The default (no `source:` field at all) means the file is the config: the surrounding `ConfigFile` is treated as inline content and consumed directly. When `source:` is present, the compiler resolves it to a config text string before parsing.

Three kinds are recognised today:

* `local` keeps the historical behaviour - the inline file is the config. This is the form that round-trips when an operator writes `source: { kind: local }` explicitly. * `git` points at a remote git repository, an optional revision (branch, tag, or commit), and a path within the repository to the actual config file. * `git_overlay` composes one base source with one or more overlay sources, merging each in order. A `db` form is reserved for a later iteration but is intentionally not part of this primitive yet.

oneOf.description: The inline file is the config; nothing is fetched. This is the historical behaviour and the implied default when `source:` is omitted.
Clone a git repository and read a single file inside it as the config text.

repo.description: Repository URL (https, ssh, or any URL `git clone` accepts).

revision.description: Optional branch, tag, or commit sha. When `None`, the default branch is used.

path.description: Path inside the repository to the config file, relative to the repository root.

oneOf.description: Compose a base source with one or more overlays. Each overlay is merged onto the accumulated result in the order it appears in the list.

base.description: The base source the overlays are layered on top of.

overlays.description: Overlays applied in order; each is itself a `ConfigSource` so overlays can chain arbitrarily deep (subject to the recursion cap enforced by the loader).

ProxyServerConfig.description:
Server-level proxy configuration parsed from the top-level `proxy:` block of sb.yml.

This is the composite home for every server-wide knob the request path reads before routing reaches an origin: listener ports, TLS / ACME sources, optional metrics and alerting, the admin API, secrets resolution, and the optional shared-state backends (L2 cache + messenger). Out-of-tree top-level blocks live in [`Self::extensions`] and are ignored by the compiler.

http2_cleartext.description:
Enable HTTP/2 cleartext (h2c) on the plain HTTP listener.

When `true`, the proxy detects the HTTP/2 connection preface on the unencrypted port and serves the connection as HTTP/2. This is required for plaintext gRPC clients that do not negotiate TLS+ALPN. When `false` (the default), the listener speaks HTTP/1.1 only and rejects raw h2 prefaces as malformed requests. TLS-fronted HTTP/2 is unaffected and continues to work via ALPN on `https_bind_port`.

https_bind_port.description: Optional HTTPS listener port. When set, `tls_cert_file` + `tls_key_file` (or `acme`) must be configured too.

tls_cert_file.description: Path to a PEM-encoded TLS certificate. Ignored when `acme` is configured.

tls_key_file.description: Path to the PEM-encoded TLS key matching `tls_cert_file`.

acme.description: ACME (Let's Encrypt-style) certificate management. When set, overrides the manual `tls_cert_file` / `tls_key_file` pair.

http3.description:
Optional HTTP/3 (QUIC) listener configuration.

Temporarily inert: HTTP/3 is disabled until native QUIC support lands in the underlying proxy engine. The field still parses so existing configs keep loading, but enabling it only logs a warning and does not start a listener.

metrics.description: Metrics collection settings, including cardinality limiting.

observability.description:
Top-level observability block: groups `log` (tracing-subscriber filter / format / sampling) and `telemetry` (OTLP exporter) under one block so an operator configures the whole surface from YAML instead of CLI flags + env vars.

When absent, CLI / env precedence still applies; the YAML fields are a third source of truth that wins over the existing `RUST_LOG` default but loses to `--log-level` / `SB_LOG_LEVEL`.

model_host.description: Canonical desired state and lifecycle policy for models hosted by SBproxy.

cluster.description: Optional shared cluster substrate for keys, metrics, and managed models.

key_management.description: Dynamic key-management configuration (WOR-1546): the mutable key store, policy cache, at-rest crypto, OIDC claim mapping, and an optional declarative seed. Distinct from the static `credentials:` block, which keeps working and lowers into the same store as config-sourced records.

l2_cache_settings.description:
Optional L2 cache / shared-state backend. When set with `driver: redis`, rate limit counters and response cache entries are stored in the external backend so multiple proxy replicas share state.

Accepted under either `l2_cache` (canonical) or `l2_cache_settings` (alias).

cache_reserve.description:
Optional Cache Reserve (long-tail cold tier) configuration.

When `enabled`, response-cache entries that pass the admission filter are mirrored to the configured backend (memory, filesystem, or Redis). On a hot miss the proxy consults the reserve before falling through to origin and promotes the entry back into the hot tier on hit.

messenger_settings.description:
Optional shared message bus for inter-component eventing (config updates, semantic-cache purges, etc.). When unset, components that need a bus degrade to no-op semantics.

YAML key: `messenger_settings`.

trusted_proxies.description:
CIDR ranges (or bare IPs) whose `X-Forwarded-For` / `X-Real-IP` / `Forwarded` headers the proxy will trust. When SBProxy is itself behind a load balancer or CDN (Cloudflare, ALB, Fly.io, ...), set this to the upstream proxy's source range so the real client IP can be recovered from the forwarding chain. Connections from any peer outside this list have their inbound forwarding headers stripped before processing, so they cannot be spoofed.

Empty by default. The TCP peer is treated as the client and no inbound forwarding metadata is honored.

correlation_id.description: Correlation-ID propagation policy. By default, the proxy honours any inbound `X-Request-Id` header, generates one if absent, forwards the value to the upstream, and echoes it in the downstream response. Set fields here to change the header name or disable.

mtls.description: Optional mTLS (mutual TLS) client certificate verification on the HTTPS listener. When set, the TLS handshake requires (or optionally accepts) a client certificate signed by the configured CA bundle. Cert metadata that Pingora exposes (organization, serial number, fingerprint) is forwarded to the upstream as `X-Client-Cert-*` headers. Requires `https_bind_port` to be set.

ai_providers_file.description: Optional override for the embedded AI provider catalog. When set, the AI gateway loads provider metadata (auth header, auth prefix, base URL, wire format, capabilities) from this YAML file at startup instead of the version compiled into the binary.

device_parser_file.description: Optional override for the embedded user-agent / device-parser regex catalog. Reserved for the (separate) UA-parser swap to a regex-driven implementation; the current pure-Rust device parser ignores this value but preserving the field shape now keeps existing sb.yml files forward-compatible.

synthetic_probe.description: Optional synthetic-transaction probe driving an in-process request through the compiled handler chain on a fixed cadence and reporting the verdict on `/readyz`. Disabled by default; opt in for deployments that want `/readyz` to fail when the proxy is unable to service its own requests.

scripting.description: Scripting runtime limits. Today this block carries the Lua sandbox knobs (execution-time budget, memory budget, pattern API gating); other languages (CEL, JavaScript, WebAssembly) keep their own knobs elsewhere until they have similar enforcement surfaces. When omitted, the documented defaults are applied (see [`LuaSandboxConfig::default`]).

extensions.description: Opaque extensions for out-of-tree top-level config blocks. The compiler never parses these; extension consumers read their own keys.

http_client_timeouts.description: Tunable client-side timeouts for the proxy's outbound HTTP helpers (forward-auth, callbacks, mirrors, SWR refreshes, bot- auth directory). Defaults match the prior hardcoded literals so existing configs see no behaviour change. See [`HttpClientTimeoutsConfig`] for the field list.

web_bot_auth.description: Web Bot Auth signing identity (WOR-805). When set, the proxy publishes the derived Ed25519 public key as an HTTP Message Signatures directory at `/.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory` so verifiers (including SBproxy's own inbound `bot_auth` directory client) can check the Web Bot Auth signatures the proxy produces. The 32-byte seed is also the key the proxy signs its outbound requests with. Absent keeps the endpoint off so existing configs are unaffected.

tenants.description:
WOR-1053: declared tenants. Each entry carries an `id` referenced by `origin.tenant_id`. Future PRs add per-tenant `credentials`, `policies`, and `vault` blocks; PR1 lands the scope so the rest of the credentials epic can land against a stable tenant resolver.

When empty, every origin resolves to the synthetic `__default__` tenant. Existing single-tenant configs see no behaviour change. An origin that names a tenant not declared here fails config compile.

credentials.description:
Canonical credentials block at proxy scope. The full schema lives in [`CredentialBlock`]. Tenant and origin scopes carry matching `credentials:` fields; resolution at request time walks origin -> tenant -> proxy, with most-restrictive policies winning across the merged set.

The legacy `virtual_keys:` YAML key under `origins[].action.providers` is rejected at config compile; operators migrate to the canonical block per `docs/migration-credentials.md`.

AcmeConfig.description: ACME (Automatic Certificate Management Environment) configuration for automatic TLS.

enabled.description: Master switch for ACME-managed TLS certificates.

email.description: Account contact email registered with the ACME directory.

directory_url.description: ACME directory URL. Defaults to Let's Encrypt production.

challenge_types.description: Allowed ACME challenge types in priority order. Defaults to `[http-01]`, the only type the proxy currently drives; `tls-alpn-01` is accepted in the list but is not yet served.

storage_backend.description: Backing store for issued certificates (`redb`, `sqlite`, etc.).

storage_path.description: Filesystem path for the certificate store.

renew_before_days.description: Number of days before expiry to attempt renewal.

Http3Config.description:
HTTP/3 (QUIC) configuration.

Temporarily inert: HTTP/3 is disabled until native QUIC support lands in the underlying proxy engine. These fields still parse, but the listener is not started; enabling it logs a warning instead.

enabled.description:
Whether to enable the HTTP/3 (QUIC) listener.

Currently ignored: HTTP/3 is temporarily disabled (see the struct docs). Setting this to `true` logs a warning and starts no listener.

max_streams.description: Maximum number of concurrent QUIC streams per connection.

idle_timeout_secs.description: Idle timeout for QUIC connections, in seconds.

max_cardinality_per_label.description: Max unique label values allowed per metric label before new values are collapsed to `__other__`. Defaults to 1 000.

hostname_cap.description: Optional override for the `hostname` label cap.

ObservabilityConfig.description: Top-level observability block: groups the `log` and `telemetry` sub-blocks so an operator can configure both from YAML rather than CLI flags + env vars. Re-uses the existing `LoggingConfig` and `TelemetryConfig` shapes from `sbproxy-observe`.

log.description: `tracing-subscriber` configuration: level, format, per-level sampling. CLI / env still wins where applicable; this block is the YAML source-of-truth for everything else.

telemetry.description: OTLP exporter configuration. When `enabled = true`, the configured endpoint receives traces and (optionally) metrics.

usage_rollups.description: Durable windowed usage rollups. On by default; omit the block to accept the defaults.

ObservabilityLogConfig.description: Subset of `sbproxy-observe::LoggingConfig` that lands in the public config schema. Kept in `sbproxy-config` so the YAML round-trips through serde without dragging a serde dependency back into the observe crate.

level.description: Log level filter. `debug | info | warn | error`. Default `info`.

redact.description: Operator-extensible redaction block. `fields` extends the built-in field-key denylist; `patterns` adds regex masks that run after the field-key pass. The built-in baseline (the hard-coded denylist in `sbproxy-observe::logging::apply_redaction`) always runs first and is not disable-able from YAML.

sinks.description:
WOR-1045: log sink fan-out (`stdout`, `stderr`, `file`, ...). When empty, the legacy single-tracing-subscriber path keeps driving stdout. Each declared sink has a unique `name` within this scope; duplicates fail config compilation. Tenant + origin sink scopes are blocked on the WOR-1051 credentials epic.

Dispatch wiring (writing each emitted line to each matching sink) lands in PR2. PR1 parses + validates the schema so an operator's e2e fixture (`e2e/tests/redaction.rs`) no longer errors at parse time.

custom_fields.description: Operator-defined custom access-log fields. Each entry adds a key to the access line's `custom` object, computed per request from either a static value with `${...}` variable interpolation or a script (CEL / Lua / JS) evaluated against the request context. Lets operators pivot logs on dimensions the built-in schema does not carry (region, deployment, a derived risk score, a hashed account id, ...) without forking the binary. Configurable at proxy, tenant, and origin scope; the sets compose per request as proxy then tenant then origin, with a more-specific scope's field overriding a less-specific field of the same `name`.

ObservabilitySamplingConfig.description: Per-level sample rates for the structured-log emitter.

ObservabilityRedactConfig.description: Operator-extensible redaction config. Sits under `proxy.observability.log.redact:` (today) and will surface at tenant and origin scopes once multi-tenant scaffolding lands.

fields.description: Additional JSON field keys whose values are replaced with `[REDACTED:<NAME>]`. Matched case-insensitively against the keys produced by `serde_json`'s renderer. Always additive on top of the built-in denylist; tenants and origins cannot disable the baseline.

patterns.description: Regex masks applied to the rendered JSON after the field-key pass. Each pattern is compiled at config-load; invalid regex is a `compile_config` error.

pii.description: Optional rule-driven PII redactor. When enabled, the global `sbproxy-security::pii::PiiRedactor` runs as a fourth pass after the value pattern scrubber, the field denylist + operator fields, and the operator regex patterns. Rules are looked up in `sbproxy-security::pii::default_rules()` by name (`email`, `credit_card`, `us_ssn`, `phone_us`, `ipv4`, `openai_key`, `anthropic_key`, `aws_access`, `github_token`, `slack_token`, `iban`).

ObservabilityRedactPattern.description: One named regex mask. `name` is reported on cardinality / counter metrics; `replacement` defaults to `[REDACTED:<NAME>]` when empty.

name.description: Operator-supplied label; appears in metrics + the marker.

replacement.description: Replacement string. Defaults to `[REDACTED:<NAME_UPPER>]` when empty; can include `$1` backrefs if the pattern has groups.

ObservabilityPiiConfig.description: Operator-controlled PII redaction at the log layer. Mirrors the per-origin `PiiConfig` used by the AI handler but applies to every emitted log line, regardless of origin.

enabled.description: Master switch. When `Some(false)`, the redactor is never built and the pipeline shorts the PII pass at this scope (and any more-specific scope that inherits without overriding). When `None`, the scope inherits its parent's `enabled` flag (proxy default is "off"); `Some(true)` turns the pass on.

rules.description: Names of the built-in rules to enable. When empty at the proxy scope, all default rules are enabled (the spirit of "PII redaction on" is the least-surprising default). At a tenant or origin scope the list is ADDED to the parent's resolved set, not replaced.

disable.description: Names of built-in rules to opt out of even when included by `rules:` or by the default-all behaviour. The matching name is case-sensitive. At a tenant or origin scope the list is SUBTRACTED from the resolved set (parent inheritance plus this scope's `rules:` additions).

ObservabilitySinkConfig.description:
WOR-1045: one declared log sink. Multiple sinks fan out from a single emit; a tenant-scoped sink only receives lines whose resolved `Principal.tenant_id` matches the tenant scope. PR1 lands the schema and uniqueness validation; dispatch wiring lands in PR2.

## Field schema

* `name` is unique within the declaring scope. The same name may appear once at proxy scope and once at tenant scope; cross-scope collisions are intentional (a tenant `acme-loki` sink is a different thing from the proxy `acme-loki` sink). * `target` selects which internal channel feeds this sink: `access_log`, `error_log`, `audit_log`, `trace_exporter`, `external_log`. The channel maps 1:1 onto the existing `sbproxy_observe::logging::Sink` enum. * `format` is the wire shape: `compact | pretty | json`. When omitted the parent `proxy.observability.log.format` decides. * `output` is the where: `stdout | stderr | file`. `otlp` lands under WOR-1046; `syslog` is a planned follow-up. * `profile` is the redaction shape: `internal` keeps JA3/JA4 and raw query strings; `external` strips them. Tenant-scoped sinks default to `external` because the operator usually does not control the downstream backend.

name.description: Unique name within the declaring scope (proxy / tenant / origin). Duplicates within a scope are rejected at config compile.

target.description: Which internal channel feeds this sink. One of `access_log | error_log | audit_log | trace_exporter | external_log`. Unknown values fail compilation.

format.description: Wire format. One of `compact | pretty | json`. Defaults to the parent `observability.log.format` when omitted.

output.description: Where the line goes. `output: { type: stdout }` keeps the legacy stdout behaviour; `file` reuses the access-log rotation stack; `otlp` lands under WOR-1046.

profile.description: Redaction profile applied to this sink's lines. One of `internal | external`. `external` strips JA3/JA4 fingerprints and raw query strings in addition to the standard redactions. Tenant-scoped sinks default to `external`.

ObservabilitySinkOutput.description:
WOR-1045 + WOR-1046: tagged-union of supported sink output types. Each variant carries its own configuration.

Variants: `stdout`, `stderr`, `file`, `otlp`. `syslog` remains a planned follow-up. Unknown `type:` values fail compilation.

oneOf.description: Write to process stdout. The default for a freshly-installed proxy.
Write to process stderr. Useful for routing the audit channel separately from access on systemd-journald.
Append to a file with optional rotation. Reuses the access-log rotation stack (`sbproxy_observe::access_log` rotation + gzip path).

path.description: Absolute path to the output file. The parent directory must exist; the file is created on first write.

max_size_mb.description: Maximum file size before rotation. Defaults to 100 MiB.

max_backups.description: Number of rotated backups to keep. Defaults to 7.

compress.description: Whether to gzip rotated backups. Defaults to true.

oneOf.description: WOR-1046: forward the rendered structured-log line to an OTLP log collector. The exporter wraps `opentelemetry_otlp::LogExporter` and ships records through a `BatchLogProcessor`. When `transport` and `timeout_secs` are omitted the sink inherits the values already declared on the top-level `telemetry:` block so a single operator config does not have to repeat collector coordinates.

endpoint.description: OTLP collector endpoint (e.g. `http://otel-collector:4318/v1/logs` for HTTP/proto, `http://otel-collector:4317` for gRPC). The path component is honoured for HTTP transport; the gRPC variant uses the host:port only.

transport.description: Transport selector: `http` or `grpc`. Defaults to whatever the top-level `telemetry.transport` declares; `grpc` when that block is absent.

timeout_secs.description: Per-export timeout in seconds. Defaults to 10 seconds when omitted; honoured by the underlying OTLP exporter's HTTP / gRPC client.

CustomLogFieldConfig.description:
One operator-defined custom access-log field.

Exactly one value source must be set: either `value` (a static string with `${...}` variable interpolation) or `source` together with `engine` (a script). Supplying both, or neither, is a config error. `engine` must be one of `cel`, `lua`, `js`. (`wasm` is rejected: it is a compiled module, not inline source.)

name.description: Key the computed value lands under in the access line's `custom` object. Must be unique within the scope.

value.description: Static value with `${...}` variable interpolation. Mutually exclusive with `source` / `engine`. Supported variables include `${env.NAME}`, `${tenant_id}`, `${method}`, `${path}`, `${host}`, `${status}`, `${provider}`, `${model}`, `${request.header.NAME}`, and `${attribution.KEY}`.

engine.description: Scripting engine for `source`. One of `cel`, `lua`, `js`. Required when `source` is set.

source.description: Script source evaluated against the request context; its result is stringified into the field. Mutually exclusive with `value`.

ObservabilityTelemetryConfig.description: Subset of `sbproxy-observe::TelemetryConfig` exposed in the YAML.

sample_rate.description: Head-based sampling probability for unsampled roots. Default 0.1.

always_sample_errors.description: Always-sample errors / policy blocks / ledger denials. Default true.

keep_over_budget_usd.description: Keep any completed trace at or above this derived USD cost.

keep_slower_than_secs.description: Keep any completed trace at or above this wall-clock latency.

resource_attrs.description: Free-form resource attributes attached to every span.

export_metrics.description: Mirror metrics over OTLP in addition to the Prometheus scrape.

metrics_interval_secs.description: Period for the OTLP metric exporter, seconds. Default 30s.

headers.description: Additional headers sent with every OTLP export request (traces, metrics, and any OTLP log sink). Values may be literals or secret references (`${VAR}`, `file:`, `vault://`, `secret://`, and the other backend URI schemes); references resolve at boot and the proxy refuses to start when one cannot be resolved, so a raw reference never reaches the collector. Hosted backends (Grafana Cloud, Honeycomb, Langfuse Cloud, Datadog) authenticate with these headers.

UsageRollupsConfig.description: Durable spend-rollup configuration (hour and day usage buckets in an embedded database, so the admin spend API serves windowed history that survives restarts). Buckets are keyed by provider, model, tenant, team, credential id, and project, and aggregate request counts, tokens, cost, and an outcome split. Rows carry no prompt content and no raw key material, so the file is safe to back up. Aggregation is deterministic.

enabled.description: Whether rollups are recorded. Defaults to `true`. When the store path cannot be opened the proxy logs a warning and runs with rollups off instead of failing boot.

retention_hourly_days.description: Days of hourly buckets to keep before compacting into daily buckets. Default 90.

retention_daily_days.description: Days of daily buckets to keep. Default 395 (about 13 months).

channels.description: List of notification channels to fire alerts to.

AlertChannelConfig.description: Configuration for a single alert notification channel.

url.description: Webhook URL. Required for `webhook` (any receiver) and `slack` (the incoming-webhook URL); unused by `pagerduty` and `log`.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers for webhook delivery.

routing_key.description: PagerDuty Events API v2 routing key (required when `channel_type == "pagerduty"`). Accepts secret references.

AdminConfig.description: Configuration for the embedded read-only admin/stats API server.

enabled.description: Whether the admin server is enabled. Defaults to false.

port.description: Port to listen on. Defaults to 9090.

username.description: HTTP Basic Auth username. Defaults to "admin".

password.description: HTTP Basic Auth password. Defaults to "changeme".

max_log_entries.description: Maximum number of recent requests to retain in the log ring buffer. Defaults to 1000.

prompt_persistence_path.description: WOR-800 PR5: filesystem path to a redb file that persists the prompt-store runtime overlay. When set, every successful `POST /admin/prompts/.../versions` and `PUT /admin/prompts/.../pin` also writes through to the file, and the file's existing contents are hydrated into the in-memory overlay at boot. Absent means PR3-style ephemeral mutations.

trace_url_template.description: URL template for trace deep-links in the admin UI. The literal `{trace_id}` is replaced with the request's trace id, e.g. `https://jaeger.internal/trace/{trace_id}`. Unset renders trace ids as plain text (no broken default link).

tls.description: Optional TLS for the admin server (WOR-1717). When set, the admin endpoint and the built-in UI are served over HTTPS using the PEM certificate and key at the configured paths, instead of plaintext HTTP. Leave unset to serve plaintext (loopback default).

bind.description: WOR-1717: address the admin server binds. Defaults to `127.0.0.1` (loopback only). Set to `0.0.0.0` or a specific interface for remote admin, and pair it with `allow_ips` and `tls`.

allow_ips.description: WOR-1717: IP / CIDR allowlist for admin clients. Empty means loopback-only (`127.0.0.1`, `::1`), the safe default. List CIDRs to permit remote admin from known networks.

cors_origins.description: WOR-1717: allowed CORS origins for the admin API, so a separately hosted SPA or dev server can call it cross-origin with credentials. Empty means no CORS headers are emitted (same-origin only).

operators.description: WOR-1716: additional admin operators with roles, for RBAC and an attributable audit trail. The top-level `username` / `password` is the implicit full-access `admin` operator; each entry here adds a read-only or admin identity that logs in with its own credentials.

AdminTlsConfig.description: TLS material for the admin server (WOR-1717): filesystem paths to a PEM certificate chain and its matching private key. Both are required together; supplying `tls` makes the admin server, including the built-in UI, serve HTTPS instead of plaintext HTTP.

cert.description: Path to the PEM certificate chain file.

key.description: Path to the PEM private key file (PKCS#8 or RSA).

AdminOperator.description: An admin operator identity with a role, for RBAC (WOR-1716).

role.description: Role governing which admin actions this operator may perform.

AdminRole.description: Admin RBAC role (WOR-1716). `read_only` may call read (GET) endpoints only; `admin` may call every admin route.

oneOf.description: Read-only: GET / read endpoints only; state-changing routes 403.
Full admin: every admin route.

store_path.description: Revision store path required by admin-managed authority.

catalog_file.description: Optional operator catalog file replacing the built-in model catalog.

max_parallel_prepares.description: Maximum artifact and engine preparations that may run concurrently.

safety_margin.description: Fraction of each device's usable memory reserved as headroom.

shutdown_deadline_ms.description: Maximum graceful drain time during shutdown.

handoff_timeout_ms.description: Maximum rolling placement handoff time while prior replicas are retained.

deployments.description: Deployment ID to desired local model.

ModelHostAuthority.description: System that owns the model-host desired state.

oneOf.description: The active `sb.yml` file is authoritative.
The authenticated admin API and its revision store are authoritative.
A configured cluster authority publishes the desired-state revision.

directory.description: Cache directory. Omission uses the platform default.

budget_gib.description: Disk budget in GiB. Omission leaves capacity operator-managed.

ManagedEngineConfig.description: Provisioning policy for one managed inference engine.

image.description: Immutable OCI image reference for container launch.

sha256.description: Expected SHA-256 for a downloaded binary or source archive.

shm_size_gib.description: Shared-memory size in GiB for container launch.

ManagedEngineLaunch.description: How a managed engine process is provisioned and launched.

oneOf.description: Resolve an installed binary or acquire a pinned binary release.
Run a digest-pinned OCI container.
Run vLLM from a managed, version-pinned uv environment.

ManagedEngineAcceleration.description: Hardware acceleration requested for a managed engine.

oneOf.description: Select from the detected worker hardware.
NVIDIA CUDA.
Apple Metal.
Vulkan.
CPU-only execution.

model.description: Logical ID from the certified model catalog.

variant.description: Exact artifact variant pin. Omission enables worker-compatible selection.

heterogeneous_variants.description: Allow replicas to choose different compatible artifact variants.

tensor_parallel.description: Fixed tensor-parallel degree per replica: the exact number of devices each replica spans. Omit to let the fit planner pick the smallest degree that fits. When set, `replicas * tensor_parallel` devices are needed, since each replica claims a distinct device set.

required_labels.description: Worker labels required by this deployment.

spread_by.description: Ordered failure-domain label keys used to spread replicas.

warm.description: Prepare the deployment during startup or reload.

cold_start.description: Behavior when no current replica is ready. Omission uses the security-profile default.

keep_alive_secs.description: Idle lifetime in seconds after the last completed request.

max_queue_depth.description: Maximum requests waiting behind active capacity.

Replicated durable state substrate configuration (WOR-1947).

Enabling this block requires `state_dir`: the replica shard persists to `<state_dir>/replicated-state.redb` so a restarted node serves its committed records from disk.

max_value_bytes.description: Maximum decoded record value size in bytes.

ClusterConsistencyLevel.description: Consistency level for replicated state reads and writes.

oneOf.description: One replica acknowledgement (the coordinator's own shard counts).
A majority of the key's replica set.
Every replica in the key's replica set.

SecretsConfig.description:
Top-level secrets management configuration.

Controls which vault backend is used to resolve `secret:` references in config values and how secret rotation is handled.

backend.description:
Backend to use for resolving secrets.

Supported values: `"env"` (default), `"local"`, `"hashicorp"`.

hashicorp.description: HashiCorp Vault connection settings. Required when `backend = "hashicorp"`.

map.description: Logical name to vault path mapping. INERT since the removal of the `secret:<name>` colon form it served (WOR-1785); still parsed for schema-v1 compatibility, and boot warns when set. Use `secret://<backend>/<name>` references instead.

fallback.description:
Fallback strategy when the vault backend is unavailable.

Supported values: `"cache"` (default), `"reject"`, `"env"`.

backends.description: Named secret backends that provider-URI references resolve against (WOR-1767). A `secret://<name>/<key>` reference resolves against the `local` backend named `<name>`; `secretfile://<name>/<key>` against the `file` backend named `<name>`. An unresolved reference in an `api_key` or `client_secret` fails startup rather than reaching the wire verbatim.

token.description: Vault token. When omitted the `VAULT_TOKEN` environment variable is used.

mount.description: KV secrets engine mount path. Defaults to `"secret"`.

grace_period_secs.description: Seconds the previous secret value remains valid after rotation. Defaults to 300 (5 minutes).

re_resolve_interval_secs.description: How often (seconds) to re-fetch secrets from the vault backend. Defaults to 60.

SecretBackendConfig.description:
One named secret backend for provider-URI resolution (WOR-1767).

Config-native (does not depend on the vault crate). The binary builds a vault manager from these at boot.

oneOf.description: In-config secrets, referenced as `secret://<name>/<key>`. Entry values may themselves be `${ENV}` so real secrets stay out of YAML.

KeyManagementConfig.description: Top-level `key_management:` block: the runtime key plane (mutable store, policy cache, governance, at-rest crypto, OIDC claim map, declarative seed).

enabled.description: Turn the dynamic key plane on. When false (default), inbound auth keeps using the compiled virtual-key registry and this block is inert.

cache.description: In-memory policy cache in front of the store.

governance.description: Governed key admission, accounting, and authenticated introspection.

allow_api_override.description: Allow the admin API to override config-seeded records on reload. When false (default), config-seeded records are authoritative and re-asserted on every reload.

failure_mode_allow.description: When the store is unreachable, allow the request through in a degraded mode. Default false: fail closed (deny).

oidc_claim_map.description: Optional OIDC/JWT claim to virtual-key mapping.

seed.description: Optional declarative seed of keys and credentials.

redis_source_of_truth.description: Treat Redis as the source of truth rather than a coherence tier (backend `redis`).

KeyStoreBackend.description: Which store backend backs the key plane.

oneOf.description: redb embedded store (default).
Redis store / coherence tier.
Secrets-manager-direct: a configured vault backend is the system of record.

mount.description: KV mount path (provider `hashicorp`) or path prefix (provider `aws`).

kv_v2.description: Use KV engine v2 (provider `hashicorp`). Default true.

token_env.description: Environment variable holding the Vault token (provider `hashicorp`). Default `VAULT_TOKEN`.

SecretsManagerProvider.description: External secrets manager backing the `secrets_manager` store backend. Only writable managers are supported (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and an in-memory `local` store for dev/tests).

oneOf.description: In-memory, non-persistent. Dev and tests only.
HashiCorp Vault KV (token auth, token read from `token_env`).
AWS Secrets Manager via the default credential chain.

max_entries.description: Soft cap on cached entries per kind (default 10000).

redis_url.description: Redis URL for the redis cache tier (when `tier: redis`). Falls back to the store URL when unset.

mesh_node_id.description: Node id for the mesh cache tier (when `tier: mesh`). Defaults to the machine hostname.

mesh.description: Mesh cluster bootstrap for the mesh cache tier. When set, the node joins a gossip cluster and the cache routes by consistent hash, so a key cached on one replica is reachable from the others. When absent, the mesh tier runs single-node.

KeyCacheTier.description: Which optional second cache tier sits behind the in-memory L1.

oneOf.description: L1 only (default).
Redis L2 tier.
Mesh distributed-cache tier.

MeshClusterConfig.description: `key_management.cache.mesh:` cluster bootstrap for the mesh cache tier.

seeds.description: Static seed peers (`host:port`) to join. An empty list bootstraps a single-node cluster.

gossip_port.description: UDP port for the gossip protocol.

transport_port.description: TCP port for the cross-node cache RPC transport. `0` requests an OS-assigned ephemeral port.

advertise_addr.description: Address this node advertises to peers (`host:port`). Defaults to the gossip bind when unset.

transport_advertise_addr.description: Address this node advertises for typed-state transport (`host:port`). Defaults to the gossip-advertised host and `transport_port`.

shared_key.description: Optional cluster-wide shared secret (AES-256-GCM) for the gossip and transport wire. Accepts an inline value or `env:NAME`. Plaintext when unset.

peer_tls.description: Optional peer mTLS (mutually-authenticated TLS) for the mesh transport. When set, inbound connections must present a CA-signed client certificate and outbound connections present this node's certificate, all verified against the configured CA. Plaintext when unset.

MeshPeerTlsConfig.description: `key_management.cache.mesh.peer_tls:` mutual-TLS material (file paths) for the mesh peer transport.

cert_file.description: Path to this node's PEM certificate chain (leaf first).

key_file.description: Path to this node's PEM private key.

ca_file.description: Path to the PEM CA certificate that signs every peer.

server_name.description: Logical server name the peer certificates are issued for (their SAN); outbound connections verify peers against it. Defaults to `sbproxy-mesh`.

KeyGovernanceConfig.description: `key_management.governance:` admission, accounting, and introspection controls for governed virtual keys.

consistency.description: Accounting consistency. Approximate is the backward-compatible default.

lease_ttl_secs.description: Reservation lease duration. Expired reservations are released when a gateway exits before settling or releasing them.

terminal_retention_secs.description: Retention for settled, released, and expired reservation outcomes. Must be at least the lease duration so retries remain idempotent.

failure_mode.description: Behavior when the governance backend cannot serve a reserve call at request time (`GovernanceError::BackendUnavailable`). The default denies the request (fail closed): governed limits must not be silently bypassed by a backend outage. Setting `allow_unreserved` admits the request instead, but every such decision is always audited on the `security_audit` channel and counted on `sbproxy_governance_fail_open_total`.

missing_rate.description: Behavior when a governed key carries a `total_micro_usd` limit but the resolved model has no rate to estimate a pre-request cost ceiling from. The default (`zero_cost`) admits with no monetary pre-gate; `require_rate` denies instead, since a limit that cannot be pre-gated must not be silently treated as unlimited.

require_governed_key.description: Require AI requests to resolve to a governed key instead of accepting origin credentials or anonymous access.

GovernanceConsistency.description: Consistency guarantee for governed key admission and accounting.

oneOf.description: Process-local atomic counters. Fast and safe on one gateway, but totals across multiple gateways are approximate.
Cluster-wide atomic reservations backed by Redis scripts.

GovernanceBackendConfig.description: Shared backend used for strict governed key accounting.

oneOf.description: A dedicated Redis connection. This is intentionally explicit and is not inherited from the key store or cache configuration.

url.description: Redis or TLS-enabled Redis connection URL.

GovernanceFailureMode.description:
Behavior when the governance backend cannot serve a reserve call (`sbproxy_ai::governance::GovernanceStore::reserve`).

Applies only to a reserve call that fails with `GovernanceError::BackendUnavailable`. Every other reserve error (invalid request shape, a reservation id reused with different input, a hit against a real governed limit, arithmetic overflow) is unrelated to backend availability and is not affected by this setting.

oneOf.description: Deny the request (`503`) when the governance backend is unavailable.

Admit the request without a governance reservation when the backend is unavailable. Always emits a `security_audit` event and increments `sbproxy_governance_fail_open_total` so the decision stays observable.

GovernanceMissingRatePolicy.description: Behavior when a governed key's monetary limit cannot be pre-gated because the resolved model has no rate to estimate a cost ceiling from.

oneOf.description: Treat the estimated cost as zero. No monetary pre-gate applies to this request; a key's `total_micro_usd` limit is still enforced at settlement, from actually billed usage.
Deny the request when the key carries a `total_micro_usd` limit but the resolved model has no rate: a limit that cannot be pre-gated must not be silently treated as unlimited.

KeyCryptoConfig.description: `key_management.crypto:` block. Both values accept a secret reference (`vault://`, `env:`, `file:`, ...) resolved at boot, or an inline value (discouraged outside tests).

pepper.description: Server pepper for inbound virtual-key hashing. When unset, a process-ephemeral pepper is generated, so stored hashes do not survive a restart; set this in production.

master_key.description: Master key for upstream-credential envelope encryption. Required to store encrypted upstream credentials; vault-ref credentials do not need it.

claim_field.description: The verified JWT/OIDC claim whose value names the virtual-key record to resolve, so the bearer-token and OIDC front doors converge on one record.

KeySeedConfig.description: `key_management.seed:` block: declarative records applied at boot.

key_id.description: Stable public id and token prefix.

secret.description: Plaintext secret hashed at boot. Mutually exclusive with `secret_hash`.

secret_hash.description: Precomputed HMAC-SHA256 hash (hex) when the operator hashed offline.

name.description: Human-readable name, surfaced on access logs.

max_tokens_per_minute.description: Max input and output tokens per minute.

max_budget_tokens.description: Max total tokens for this key's budget window.

max_budget_usd.description: Max total cost in USD for this key's budget window.

allowed_models.description: Models this key may use (empty = all).

blocked_models.description: Models this key may not use.

allowed_providers.description: Providers this key may use (empty = all).

blocked_providers.description: Providers this key may not use. Blocks override allows.

allowed_tools.description: Caller-supplied tool allowlist. `None` is unrestricted and an empty list denies every caller-supplied tool.

require_pii_redaction.description: Named PII redaction rules that must be active before this key can dispatch upstream (empty = none required).

principal_selectors.description: Inbound principal selectors allowed to present this key (empty = any). Each entry is a principal-selector object (virtual_key, team, project, user, role, claim).

route_to_model.description: Pin a model for requests on this key; the gateway overwrites the request body `model` before routing.

compression_profile.description: Route-local compression selector (`on`, `off`, or a named profile).

inject_tools.description: Provider tool definitions injected into the request when this key authenticates, replacing any client-supplied tools.

inject_mcp.description: Federated MCP catalogue reference injected for this key.

bypass_prompt_injection.description: Skip the body-aware prompt-injection scan for this key. Default false.

metadata.description: Free-form string metadata for audit and usage attribution.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 expiry instant; past it the key is unusable.

vault_ref.description: A secret reference (`vault://`, `awssm://`, ...). Stored as vault-ref material and resolved at use.

secret.description: A plaintext secret to envelope-encrypt at boot (needs `crypto.master_key`).

L2CacheConfig.description:
Top-level shared-state / L2 cache backend configuration.

Turns rate-limit buckets and response-cache entries into cluster-wide shared state so multiple proxy replicas coordinate against the same counters and cache pool. YAML key: `l2_cache_settings`.

driver.description: Backend driver. Currently only `"redis"` is supported.

L2CacheParams.description:
Driver-specific parameters for the [`L2CacheConfig`].

Kept separate from `L2CacheConfig` so future drivers can add fields (auth, pool size) without churning the parent struct.

dsn.description: Redis connection DSN. Supports `redis://`, `rediss://`, credentials, bracketed IPv6 addresses, and a non-negative logical database.

ca_file.description: Optional path to PEM-encoded Redis trust anchors for a private CA.

cert_file.description: Optional path to a PEM-encoded Redis client certificate chain. Must be configured together with `key_file` and requires `rediss://`.

key_file.description: Optional path to the PEM-encoded Redis client private key. Must be configured together with `cert_file` and requires `rediss://`.

CacheReserveConfig.description:
Top-level Cache Reserve configuration.

Cache Reserve is a long-tail cold tier sitting under the per-origin response cache. Items evicted from the hot cache are admitted into the reserve subject to a sample rate and size threshold; on a hot miss the proxy consults the reserve before going to origin and promotes the entry back into the hot tier on hit.

Backend selection is open-ended via [`CacheReserveBackendConfig`] so the in-tree memory / filesystem / redis backends can be extended without touching this schema.

enabled.description: Master switch. When `false`, the reserve is not built and the hot cache behaves exactly as it does without this block.

sample_rate.description: Fraction (0.0 to 1.0) of hot-cache writes that are mirrored to the reserve. Defaults to `0.1`. The reserve is meant for long-tail content; sampling controls reserve write amplification and (on object-store backends) per-request operation cost.

min_ttl.description: Skip mirroring entries whose TTL is below this threshold. Items that won't outlive a typical hot-cache eviction window aren't worth carrying in the reserve. Defaults to 3600 seconds.

max_size_bytes.description: Skip oversize objects. Defaults to 1 MiB. Set to `0` to disable the upper bound (not recommended for object-store backends).

CacheReserveBackendConfig.description:
Backend selector for [`CacheReserveConfig`].

Tagged externally on `type`. The built-in variants are listed below; out-of-tree builds may register additional types via their own startup path (the in-tree pipeline ignores unknown types after logging a warning).

oneOf.description: In-process map. For tests and ephemeral single-replica setups.
Filesystem-backed reserve. One file per key under `path`.

path.description: Root directory under which entries are written.

oneOf.description: Redis-backed reserve.

key_prefix.description: Optional key prefix override. Defaults to `"sbproxy:reserve:"`.

oneOf.description: Catch-all for backends registered out-of-tree (e.g. an `s3` backend). The in-tree pipeline ignores these with a warning; an out-of-tree startup hook intercepts the variant before the warning fires.

MessengerSettings.description:
Configuration for the shared message bus used by inter-component events (config updates, semantic-cache purges, etc.).

The `driver` selects the backend implementation: * `"memory"` - in-process bounded mpsc channels (single-replica use only). * `"redis"`  - Redis Streams over the DSN in `params.dsn`. * `"sqs"`    - AWS SQS; requires `params.queue_url`, `params.region`, `params.api_key`. * `"gcp_pubsub"` - GCP Pub/Sub; requires `params.project`, `params.topic`, `params.subscription`, `params.access_token`.

Unknown drivers cause `build_messenger` to return an error; the caller decides whether to treat that as fatal or fall back to no-bus semantics.

params.description: Free-form string parameters consumed by the driver-specific factory.

CorrelationIdConfig.description:
Correlation-ID propagation policy.

The proxy mints a per-request correlation identifier early in the request lifecycle. With the default policy:

1. If the inbound request carries `header` (default `X-Request-Id`), its value is adopted as the request's correlation ID. This lets upstream callers (a frontend, an API client, another proxy) correlate their traces with ours. 2. Otherwise the proxy generates a 32-hex-character UUID v4 and uses that. 3. The chosen value is set on the upstream request (under the same header name) so the upstream sees the same correlation ID the proxy used in its logs / webhooks. 4. The chosen value is echoed back to the client on the response, unless `echo_response` is `false`.

header.description: Header name to read on ingress, set on the upstream, and echo on the response. Default: `X-Request-Id`.

echo_response.description: Whether to echo the correlation ID on the downstream response. Default: `true`.

MtlsListenerConfig.description:
mTLS client certificate verification on the HTTPS listener.

When set, the proxy configures the OpenSSL `SslAcceptor` underneath Pingora's `add_tls_with_settings` to verify the client certificate against the configured CA bundle.

What we expose to the upstream after a successful handshake: * `X-Client-Cert-Verified: 1` * `X-Client-Cert-Organization: <Subject's O field, when present>` * `X-Client-Cert-Serial: <hex serial>` * `X-Client-Cert-Fingerprint: <hex sha256 of the cert>`

CN and SAN extraction is a follow-up because Pingora 0.8's `SslDigest` does not expose the parsed Subject CN directly. When `require: true`, requests without a valid client cert are rejected during the TLS handshake and never reach `request_filter`.

client_ca_file.description: Path to a PEM-encoded CA bundle used to verify client certs.

require.description: When `true` (default), the TLS handshake fails if the client does not present a certificate. When `false`, the handshake succeeds without a cert and `X-Client-Cert-Verified: 0` is set (so upstreams can choose whether to reject anonymous traffic).

allowed_cn_patterns.description: Optional allowlist of regex patterns the client certificate's Common Name must match. When non-empty, a certificate that passes CA-chain validation is still rejected during the handshake if its CN matches none of these patterns. Empty (the default) accepts any CN signed by the configured CA (WOR-1155).

SyntheticProbeConfig.description:
Configuration for the in-process synthetic-transaction probe.

When enabled, a background task fires a request through the compiled pipeline against the configured `hostname` on a fixed cadence. The request never leaves the process: the synthetic origin is required to use a non-network action (typically `static`, `mock`, `echo`, or `noop`) so `/readyz` can verify the handler chain end to end without making the readiness check dependent on a real upstream.

The probe verdict is reported as a `synthetic_pipeline` component in the `/readyz` body and increments `sbproxy_synthetic_probe_failures_total{reason}` whenever the driver records a failure.

enabled.description: Master switch. Disabled by default so operators with strict request-cost budgets do not pay for a synthetic transaction they did not opt into.

hostname.description: Sentinel hostname routed to the synthetic origin. Defaults to `__synthetic.local` per the synthetic-probe convention; pick another value if it collides with an existing origin in your deployment.

path.description: Path issued on the synthetic request. Defaults to `/readyz/synthetic`.

timeout_ms.description: Per-run timeout budget. The driver records a `timeout` failure if a single synthetic round trip exceeds this.

stale_after_secs.description: Maximum age (in seconds) the cached probe outcome can have before the readiness probe reports `Unhealthy`. Set this to roughly 3x `interval_secs`. Defaults to `interval_secs * 3` when zero.

ScriptingConfig.description:
Per-engine scripting sandbox limits, exposed under the `proxy.scripting:` block of sb.yml.

Today this block carries sub-blocks for the Lua engine and the JavaScript engine. The CEL and WebAssembly engines manage their own budgets separately. Operators who omit the block get the documented defaults from each sub-block.

lua.description: Lua sandbox limits. Always populated, even when the operator omitted the block, so callers never have to special-case `None`.

javascript.description: JavaScript engine sandbox knobs. Covers the QuickJS-backed `JsEngine` used by transforms, request matchers, and WAF custom rules.

LuaScriptingConfig.description: Lua scripting runtime configuration. Wraps the sandbox limits so future Lua-specific tunables (preloaded libraries, request-binding budgets, etc.) have a stable home.

LuaSandboxConfig.description:
Sandbox configuration applied to every Lua script invocation.

Three knobs:

* `max_execution_ms` is a wall-clock budget enforced through the Luau interrupt callback. Once exceeded, the script is aborted with an `Error::external` propagated back to the caller. * `max_memory_mb` caps the Lua VM's total allocator footprint. Allocations past the limit fail the script with `Error::MemoryError`, which is far cheaper than letting a runaway script OOM the proxy process. * `allow_patterns` gates the Lua pattern API (`string.find`, `string.match`, `string.gmatch`). The pattern engine has known pathological inputs that can lock a worker, so operators who do not need patterns can drop them entirely.

The on-the-wire field uses `max_memory_mb` (megabytes) because that is the unit operators reason about; the engine converts to bytes internally.

max_execution_ms.description: Wall-clock execution budget per Lua invocation, in milliseconds. Default: 100 ms.

max_memory_mb.description: Hard cap on the Lua VM's allocator footprint, in megabytes. Default: 8 MB.

allow_patterns.description: Whether to expose the Lua pattern API (`string.find`, `string.match`, `string.gmatch`). Default: `true` for back compatibility; flip to `false` to disable pattern matching.

JsScriptingConfig.description:
JavaScript engine config block (`proxy.scripting.javascript:`).

Wraps the sandbox limits the engine enforces every time it runs a script. Adding fresh knobs here (module loader settings, host bindings, ...) should keep `sandbox:` as its own sub-block so existing configs keep parsing.

sandbox.description: Sandbox limits: CPU time budget, heap memory cap, and native stack cap. See [`JsSandboxConfig`].

JsSandboxConfig.description:
JavaScript sandbox limits enforced on every script execution.

The `budget_ms` field is the CPU time budget for a single `execute` / `call_function` / `match_request` / `waf_match` call. QuickJS calls the engine's interrupt handler periodically during evaluation; when the elapsed wall-clock time exceeds `budget_ms` the interrupt handler returns `true`, which aborts the script with an uncatchable exception that surfaces in Rust as a structured timeout error.

The `memory_mb` and `stack_kb` fields are passed through to `Runtime::set_memory_limit` and `Runtime::set_max_stack_size` respectively. They guard against runaway allocations and deeply recursive scripts in the same way the CPU budget guards against `while (true) {}`.

budget_ms.description: Wall-clock CPU budget per script execution. Defaults to 100 ms, which is comfortably above any reasonable transform / matcher script but well under the per-request timeout budget of a typical request.

memory_mb.description: Maximum heap memory the QuickJS runtime is allowed to allocate for the lifetime of this engine instance. Defaults to 16 MB.

stack_kb.description: Maximum native stack size for the QuickJS runtime, in kilobytes. Defaults to 1024 KB (1 MB).

HttpClientTimeoutsConfig.description:
Tunable client-side timeouts for the proxy's outbound HTTP helpers.

Several internal code paths build pooled `reqwest::Client` instances to call out to operator-controlled services: forward-auth services, callback / webhook receivers, mirror destinations, stale-while- revalidate refreshes against origin upstreams, and Web Bot Auth directory lookups. Each helper used to bake a `Duration::from_secs` literal into a `LazyLock`-built client, which meant operators had to fork the binary to extend a timeout for a slow auth service or shorten one for an aggressive deadline budget.

All fields default to the prior hardcoded values so existing configs see no behaviour change. Operators only set a field here to nudge a specific timeout.

Example:

```yaml proxy: http_client_timeouts: forward_auth_client_secs: 60 callback_client_secs: 15 ```

forward_auth_client_secs.description: Outer client-level timeout for the shared forward-auth `reqwest::Client`. The per-request timeout from each `forward_auth.timeout` field still applies on top. Defaults to 30s.

forward_auth_request_secs.description: Per-request fallback timeout for a forward-auth subrequest when the auth provider's own `timeout` field is unset. Defaults to 5s.

bot_auth_directory_client_secs.description: Client-level timeout for the Web Bot Auth directory client that fetches signed directories from agent operators. Defaults to 5s.

swr_client_secs.description: Client-level timeout for the stale-while-revalidate refresh client that re-fetches expired cache entries in the background. Defaults to 30s to match the conservative outer ceiling the rest of the proxy uses for outbound HTTP.

callback_client_secs.description: Client-level timeout for the callback / webhook client that fires audit-mode webhooks and other fire-and-forget POSTs. Defaults to 10s.

WebBotAuthConfig.description:
Web Bot Auth signing identity for the proxy. See the [`ProxyServerConfig::web_bot_auth`] field.

The proxy holds one Ed25519 keypair, identified by `key_id`. Its public half is published in the hosted signatures directory; its private seed signs outbound requests to upstreams that require Web Bot Auth. Treat `ed25519_seed_hex` as a secret (source it via an env interpolation rather than committing it).

key_id.description: Key id advertised as the JWK `kid` and the RFC 9421 `keyid`. Must be non-empty.

ed25519_seed_hex.description: Ed25519 private seed as 64 hex characters (32 bytes). The public key is derived and published; the seed never leaves the proxy. Validated at config-compile time.

directory_url.description: Absolute URL of this proxy's published key directory (`/.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory`). When set, an origin that opts into outbound Web Bot Auth signing stamps a `Signature-Agent` header with this value so the upstream verifier can fetch the key. Optional: omitting it still signs, just without the discovery pointer.

ProxyTenantConfig.description:
WOR-1053: declared tenant. PR1 only carries the `id`; PR2+ adds per-tenant `credentials`, `policies`, `vault`, and `observability` blocks alongside the multi-tenant inheritance fan-out.

A reserved tenant id of `__default__` is the synthetic default every origin resolves to when `origin.tenant_id` is absent. The operator never declares `__default__` explicitly; doing so fails config compile.

id.description: Operator-supplied stable identifier. Referenced from `origin.tenant_id` and stamped on every request the origin serves. Length capped to 256 ASCII characters at compile.

credentials.description: Tenant-scoped credentials block. Inherits proxy-scope credentials of the same name unless overridden here.

observability.description: Tenant-scoped observability block. Today the only nested surface is `log.redact.pii`, which composes against the proxy-scope `observability.log.redact.pii` block (see [`ObservabilityPiiConfig`]). Origin-scope and proxy-scope values compose in the same shape; resolution at emit time walks origin -> tenant -> proxy with most-specific-wins on `enabled` and a rules set that inherits + extends + disables. Absent leaves the tenant inheriting whatever proxy scope declared (or no PII pass at all).

CredentialBlock.description:
Canonical credentials block. Sits under `proxy.credentials`, `tenants[].credentials`, or `origins[].credentials`. A request resolves matching credentials by walking origin -> tenant -> proxy scopes; the first scope that produces a match for the request's principal serves the credential.

The credential carries:

* Which provider produces it (`type`, `provider`). * Where the secret material lives (`key`, a provider-specific secret reference such as `vault://`, `awssm://`, `gcpsm://`, `k8ssecret://`, `secretfile://`, or `secret://`, or a legacy `${ENV}` / `file:` / `secret:` reference). * Which inbound principals can use it (`principals` selectors). * Per-credential attribution metadata (`attrs`). * Allow / deny model lists that stack on top of the origin-level allowlist (most-restrictive wins). * Per-credential sub-policies (rate limit, PII redaction, ...).

name.description: Operator-supplied stable name. Unique within the declaring scope. Used to identify the credential in metrics and logs.

provider.description: Provider name for `type: ai_provider` credentials. Matches an entry in the origin's `providers:` list. Ignored for non-AI credential kinds.

key.description: Secret material reference. Provider-specific schemes include `vault://`, `awssm://`, `gcpsm://`, `k8ssecret://`, `secretfile://`, and `secret://`; legacy `${ENV}`, `file:`, and `secret:` forms also remain valid. The resolver dispatches at runtime; the config parser carries it as a string.

principals.description: Principal selectors that match this credential to inbound principals. An empty list matches every principal; downstream resolution then uses the first credential whose selectors match the request.

attrs.description: Attribution attributes copied onto matched principals.

models.description: Model allow / deny lists. Stacks on top of the origin-level allowlist (most-restrictive wins).

policies.description: Sub-policies that only fire when this credential matches.

route_to_model.description: Pin the upstream `model` field. When set, the AI dispatch rewrites the request's `model` before sending it to the provider; the client-supplied value is ignored. Mirrors the `route_to_model` field on the underlying `VirtualKeyConfig`.

compression_profile.description: Select `on`, `off`, or a named route-local compression profile.

inject_tools.description: Replace the request's `tools` array with these entries. The shape is provider-native (`function` objects today); the AI dispatch forwards the array verbatim. Empty == no injection. Mirrors `inject_tools` on the underlying `VirtualKeyConfig`.

allowed_tools.description: Caller-supplied tool allowlist for this credential. Omitted is unrestricted and an explicit empty list denies every caller tool.

inject_mcp.description: WOR-1646: inject a federated MCP gateway's live catalogue as this credential's tool surface. Raw passthrough of the `InjectMcpRef` shape (`{ref, format, filter}`) on the underlying `VirtualKeyConfig`; resolved at request time.

PrincipalSelector.description: Selector matching an inbound principal to a credential. At least one field must be set; an entirely empty selector is rejected at compile.

virtual_key.description: Glob matching `Principal.virtual_key.name`. `*` matches any virtual key. `vk_frontend_*` matches every key with that prefix.

claim.description: Match an exact key=value entry on `Principal.attrs.claims`. Serialised as a flat map for readability.

CredentialAttrs.description: Attribution attributes copied onto matched principals.

project.description: Project the credential's spend rolls up to.

user.description: User the credential is owned by (independent of who the inbound request authenticates as).

team.description: Team grouping. Drives the team partition on the per-credential attribution metric.

cost_center.description: Cost center. Lifted onto `Principal.attrs.metadata` under the `cost_center` key for back-compat with the existing access-log surface.

tags.description: Operator-supplied tags. Each tag becomes a separate attribution row.

budget.description: Per-credential budget. Sits inside `attrs:` because budget is an attribution-side concern; the budget enforcer reads the matched principal's attrs to apply caps.

CredentialBudget.description: Per-credential budget. Reset windows use the LiteLLM-style `30s|30m|30h|30d` syntax.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens (input + output combined) per reset window.

max_cost_usd.description: Maximum USD spend per reset window.

CredentialModels.description: Model allow / deny lists scoped to this credential. Stacks on top of the origin-level allowlist. Most-restrictive wins.

allow.description: Models this credential is allowed to use. Empty allows all origin-allowed models.

deny.description: Models this credential is explicitly denied. Stacks on top of `allow`: a model that is in `allow` but also in `deny` is denied.

CredentialPolicy.description: Sub-policy attached to a credential. Closed enum; out-of-tree policies plug in through the existing plugin registry rather than widening this enum.

oneOf.description: Per-credential rate limit. Stacks on top of the origin-level rate limit (most-restrictive wins).
Require PII redaction for the named rule set on every request served by this credential. The names match `sbproxy_security::pii::default_rules`.

rules.description: Rule names that MUST run on every request.

TenantObservabilityConfig.description: Tenant-scope observability sub-tree. The `log.redact` block (PII rules, patterns, and field denylist), `log.sinks` (tenant-scoped fan-out, filtered by `Principal.tenant_id`), `log.custom_fields` (tenant-scoped access-log fields that override proxy-scope fields of the same name), and `cardinality` (per-tenant metric label budget) are all consumed at runtime.

cardinality.description: WOR-1067: per-tenant cardinality budget. Caps the unique label value count across `sbproxy_requests_total` and friends for just this tenant so a noisy tenant cannot demote labels for every other tenant. Omitting the block leaves this tenant on the proxy-wide budget (today's behaviour).

TenantObservabilityLogConfig.description: Tenant-scope `log:` sub-block. Mirrors the proxy-scope `ObservabilityLogConfig`; today exposes the redaction leaf plus the tenant-scoped sinks fan-out (WOR-1045 PR2). The dispatcher routes every record whose resolved `Principal.tenant_id` matches this tenant into each declared sink; cross-tenant records never reach here.

sinks.description: Tenant-scoped log sinks. Each sink's `name` is unique within this tenant; the same name may also appear at proxy scope (they are different sinks). Sinks at this scope default to the `external` redaction profile because the downstream backend is usually outside the operator's trust boundary.

custom_fields.description: Tenant-scoped custom access-log fields. Same shape as the proxy-scope `custom_fields:`. A field defined here overrides a proxy-scope field with the same `name` for requests resolved to this tenant; an origin-scope field overrides both.

TenantObservabilityRedactConfig.description: Tenant-scope `redact:` sub-block. Today only `pii:` is honoured; the field-key and pattern overrides remain proxy-scope only because they touch the rendered JSON, which is tenant-agnostic in the emitter.

fields.description: WOR-1042: tenant-scope additions to the field-key denylist. Additive only; a tenant CANNOT disable a proxy-level field denylist entry because the security baseline always applies.

patterns.description: WOR-1042: tenant-scope additions to the regex pattern set. Additive on top of the proxy-scope patterns. Use `disable:` (below) to opt out of a more-general proxy-scope pattern by name (e.g. a healthcare tenant disabling a `phone_us` mask).

disable.description: WOR-1042: names of proxy-scope `patterns:` entries to opt out of at this tenant. Targets only the operator-supplied regex pass; the built-in field-key denylist is never disable-able.

pii.description: Tenant-scope override for the proxy-scope PII pass. Resolution rules: the tenant inherits the proxy-scope `enabled` flag and the proxy-scope rule set, then ADDS its own `rules:` entries and SUBTRACTS its own `disable:` entries. An explicit `enabled: false` opts the tenant out even when proxy-scope enables PII. See [`ObservabilityPiiConfig`].

TenantCardinalityConfig.description: WOR-1067: per-tenant cardinality budget. The runtime installs one dedicated label-value tracker per declared tenant; overflows on tenant B do not touch tenant A's accepted-value set. The `__default__` tenant continues to use the proxy-wide `CardinalityLimiter` (in `sbproxy-observe`) so single-tenant deployments stay bit-for-bit identical to pre-WOR-1067 behaviour.

max_series.description: Maximum unique label values per metric, per label name, for requests resolving to this tenant. When omitted the observability stack falls back to its per-tenant default cap ([`crate::types::TENANT_CARDINALITY_DEFAULT_MAX_SERIES`]) so an operator can declare an `observability.cardinality:` block with no fields to opt this tenant in to the default per-tenant budget without having to pick a number.

RawOriginConfig.description: A single origin config as it appears in YAML. Plugin-specific fields are kept as `serde_json::Value` for deferred parsing.

action.description: Action describing what the origin does (proxy, redirect, static, etc.).

tenant_id.description: WOR-1053: declared tenant for this origin. Must match an `id` under `proxy.tenants[]`; absent resolves to the synthetic `__default__` tenant so existing single-tenant configs keep working unchanged.

credentials.description: Canonical credentials block at origin scope. Overrides + adds to the tenant + proxy scopes. See [`CredentialBlock`].

authentication.description: Authentication block (also accepted under YAML alias `auth`).

policies.description: Policy entries (rate limit, WAF, IP filter, etc.) evaluated in order.

transforms.description: Transform pipeline applied to request and response bodies.

request_modifiers.description: Request modifiers (header rewrites, path edits, etc.).

response_modifiers.description: Response modifiers applied after the action returns.

session.description: Optional session cookie configuration (also accepted as `session_config`).

properties.description: Per-origin custom-properties capture. Mirrors [`sbproxy_observe::PropertiesConfig`]; absent means the proxy uses the type's `Default` (capture on, no echo, no redaction).

sessions.description: Per-origin session-id capture. Mirrors [`sbproxy_observe::SessionsConfig`]; absent means the proxy uses the type's `Default` (capture on, `Anonymous` auto-generate).

user.description: Per-origin user-id capture. Mirrors [`sbproxy_observe::UserConfig`]; absent means the proxy uses the type's `Default` (capture on, 256-byte cap).

force_ssl.description: When true, redirect plain HTTP requests to HTTPS.

allowed_methods.description: Whitelist of HTTP methods this origin accepts; empty allows all.

forward_rules.description: Path-based forward rules to inline child origins.

fallback_origin.description: Origin used when the primary upstream fails.

variables.description: Static variables available for template interpolation.

on_request.description: Hooks invoked when a request enters the origin.

on_response.description: Hooks invoked when a response is being returned.

error_pages.description: Per-status custom error response bodies. Each entry covers one or more HTTP status codes and contributes a content-typed body the proxy substitutes when it generates the matching status. Multiple entries for the same status are content-negotiated against the inbound request's `Accept` header.

problem_details.description: RFC 9457 `application/problem+json` default-renderer configuration. When enabled, proxy-generated errors that are not matched by an [`ErrorPageEntry`] render as a structured problem-details body. Composes with `error_pages`: custom pages still win when authored. See [`ProblemDetailsConfig`].

proxy_status.description: RFC 9209 `Proxy-Status` response header configuration. When enabled, the proxy stamps a structured `Proxy-Status` header on every non-2xx response so downstream clients can diagnose forwarding errors without scraping the body. The identity token defaults to `sbproxy` and can be overridden for fleet- wide branding (e.g. `acme-edge`).

mirror.description: Shadow traffic mirror, fire-and-forget copy of each request to a separate upstream. See [`MirrorConfig`].

olp.description: WOR-808 PR7: RSL Open License Protocol issuer configuration. When set, the proxy serves `/.well-known/olp/token` (issuance) and `/.well-known/olp/key` (JWK publication) on the origin so crawlers following a `WWW-Authenticate: License` challenge can obtain and verify license tokens.

web_bot_auth_publish.description: WOR-805 AC#4: opt in to publishing SBproxy's own Web Bot Auth signing-key directory at `/.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory` and the Signature Agent Card discovery doc. Verifiers that fetch the directory can then verify the signatures SBproxy attaches to outbound requests when the corresponding `MessageSignatureSigner` runs upstream of the proxy.

idempotency.description: RFC 8594-style idempotency-key middleware. Opt in per origin to have the proxy short-circuit retries of POST/PUT/PATCH (or any configured method) carrying a repeated `Idempotency-Key` header. See [`IdempotencyConfig`].

connection_pool.description: Per-origin connection pool tuning.  Falls back to proxy-wide defaults when not specified.

extensions.description:
Opaque per-origin extensions for out-of-tree config blocks.

The compiler never parses these values. Extension consumers (e.g. a semantic-cache hook) read their own nested keys by name. Mirrors the server-level `proxy.extensions` pattern so the schema stays neutral.

expose_openapi.description: When true, the gateway exposes a per-host OpenAPI document at `/.well-known/openapi.json` (and `.yaml`) for this origin. Off by default: emission is opt-in so origins do not leak route shape without the operator's consent.

stream_safety.description: Per-origin streaming safety rule identifiers. Forwarded to the stream-safety hook so each origin can enforce its own subset (e.g. `["pii", "toxicity"]`). Empty disables streaming safety for the origin even when the hook is wired.

default_content_shape.description: Per-origin default content shape used when the agent's `Accept` header is `*/*` or absent. Threaded into the synthesised `auto_content_negotiate` config by [`crate::compile_origin`]. Recognised values: `markdown`, `json`, `html`, `pdf`, `other`. Unset falls back to `html`.

content_signal.description: Per-origin `Content-Signal` response header value. Closed enum (validated at compile time): `ai-train`, `search`, `ai-input`. When set, the proxy stamps `Content-Signal: <value>` on 200 responses for this origin and the projection cache (`licenses.xml`, `tdmrep.json`) reflects the same signal. An unset value means "no signal asserted" and the proxy stamps `TDM-Reservation: 1` instead.

token_bytes_ratio.description: Per-origin override for the Markdown projection's tokens-per-byte ratio. Threads into the synthesised `html_to_markdown` transform's `token_bytes_ratio` field and the projection fallback path so the `x-markdown-tokens` response header and the JSON envelope's `token_estimate` field both honour the override. Unset falls back to `DEFAULT_TOKEN_BYTES_RATIO` (0.25).

agent_skills.description: Per-origin Agent Skills v0.2.0 advertisement. When non-empty, the proxy serves `GET /.well-known/agent-skills/index.json` for this origin and re-hosts each path-absolute or relative artifact at the URL declared in the entry. Empty (or absent) keeps the well-known endpoint disabled for the origin so v1 configs compile unchanged.

agents_md.description: Per-origin `/AGENTS.md` body (WOR-809). When set, the proxy serves it verbatim at `GET /AGENTS.md` (content type `text/markdown`) per the AGENTS.md agent-instructions convention. Independent of `ai_crawl_control`. Absent keeps the endpoint off.

ai_txt.description: Per-origin `/ai.txt` body (WOR-809). When set, the proxy serves it verbatim at `GET /ai.txt` per the Spawning ai.txt convention. Independent of `ai_crawl_control`. Absent keeps the endpoint off.

agents_json.description: Per-origin agents.json manifest (WOR-820). When set, the proxy serves `GET /.well-known/agents.json` (the Wildcard agents.json v0.1 spec): operator-authored `info` + `flows`, with `sources` defaulting to the origin's emitted OpenAPI document. Independent of `ai_crawl_control`. Absent keeps the endpoint off.

outbound_credential.description: Per-origin outbound credential resolver (WOR-802). When set, the proxy mints/resolves a credential and stamps it on the request it sends upstream (RFC 8693 token exchange, OAuth client-credentials, or a vault-resolved secret). Kept as JSON for deferred compilation in `sbproxy-core` (the typed enum lives in `sbproxy-modules`). Secret fields use the standard `${ENV}` interpolation, resolved at config load.

outbound_web_bot_auth.description: Opt this origin into outbound Web Bot Auth signing (WOR-805). When `true` and `proxy.web_bot_auth` is configured, the proxy signs the request it sends upstream with the proxy's Ed25519 key (RFC 9421, `tag=web-bot-auth`), so an upstream that demands Web Bot Auth accepts SBproxy as a verified agent. Default `false`.

observability.description: Origin-scope observability block. Today the only nested surface is `log.redact.pii`, which composes against the tenant-scope block (or proxy-scope when the origin has no tenant). See [`OriginObservabilityConfig`].

RequestModifierConfig.description:
Request modifier entry.
