Each modifier entry can contain one or more of: `headers`, `url`, `query`, `method`, `body`, or `lua_script`. Multiple modifier entries in the list are applied in order.

lua_script.description: Optional Lua script for dynamic request modification.

js_script.description: Optional JavaScript script for dynamic request modification.

set.description: Headers to set, replacing any existing value.

PathRewrite.description: Path rewrite: replace a substring in the path.

replace.description: Replace a substring in the path.

PathReplace.description: A simple string-replace operation on the URL path.

add.description: Add query parameters (appended even if the key already exists).

BodyModifier.description: Body replacement configuration for request modifiers.

replace_json.description: Replace the request body with this JSON value.

replace.description: Replace the request body with this string.

ResponseModifierConfig.description:
Response modifier entry.

Each modifier entry can contain one or more of: `headers`, `status`, `body`, or `lua_script`. Multiple modifier entries in the list are applied in order.

status.description: Override the response status code and optional reason text.

lua_script.description: Optional Lua script for dynamic response modification.

js_script.description: Optional JavaScript script for dynamic response modification.

StatusOverride.description: Status code override for response modifiers.

code.description: The HTTP status code to set.

text.description: Optional reason phrase (not sent in HTTP/2, informational only).

ResponseBodyModifier.description: Body replacement configuration for response modifiers.

replace.description: Replace the response body with this string.

replace_json.description: Replace the response body with this JSON value.

max_age.description: Optional preflight cache duration in seconds (`Access-Control-Max-Age`).

enable.description: Legacy `enable: true` flag (alias: `enabled`). Accepted but not checked at runtime because the presence of the cors block is sufficient.

max_age.description: `max-age` directive of the `Strict-Transport-Security` header, in seconds.

include_subdomains.description: When true, emits the `includeSubDomains` HSTS directive.

preload.description: When true, emits the `preload` HSTS directive.

enabled.description: Master switch for response compression. Alias: `enable`.

min_size.description: Minimum response size, in bytes, before compression is applied.

level.description: Compression level. Reserved; not currently honored by the runtime.

http_only.description: When true, sets the `HttpOnly` cookie attribute.

secure.description: When true, sets the `Secure` cookie attribute (HTTPS only).

allow_non_ssl.description: When true, allow sessions over non-SSL connections.

PropertiesConfig.description: Per-origin properties capture configuration. Defaults match the "capture on, no echo, no redaction" baseline; operators tune via `sb.yml`.

capture.description: Master switch. When `false`, [`capture_properties`] returns an empty map regardless of headers.

echo.description: Echo captured properties back as `X-Sb-Property-<key>` response headers. Off by default; opt-in per origin.

redact.description: Redaction rules applied after capture and before any subscriber observes the event.

RedactConfig.description: Redaction rules for captured property values.

keys.description: Exact-match keys whose values are replaced with `"[redacted]"`. Match is case-insensitive on the lowercased captured key.

value_regex.description: Value-content regex patterns. Any value matching any pattern is replaced wholesale with `"[redacted]"`.

capture.description: Master capture switch. When `false`, callers see no session ID regardless of headers or auto-generation policy.

auto_generate.description: When to auto-generate a session ID for callers that did not supply one.

ttl_seconds.description: Sessions index TTL in seconds. The proxy itself does not enforce TTL; this knob feeds the ClickHouse projections (T2.4) which key storage on `(workspace_id, session_id)`.

budget.description: Cap on auto-generated session IDs per workspace per window. `None` disables the gate (default). When the cap is hit, the dimension is dropped silently and `sbproxy_capture_budget_dropped_total{workspace,dimension="session"}` is incremented. Caller-supplied session IDs from `X-Sb-Session-Id` are never gated.

AutoGenerate.description: Auto-generation policy for session IDs when the caller does not supply one.

oneOf.description: Never mint a session ID. Capture caller-supplied IDs only.

Mint a session ID only when the caller is anonymous (no resolved user identity). This is the default for AI gateway origins; bursts of anonymous traffic get one session per burst rather than one per request.
Always mint a session ID when one is not supplied.

BudgetConfig.description:
Per-dimension cap that the capture helpers consult before stamping auto-generated session IDs or admitting user IDs onto the request envelope. Counts are kept per workspace and rotate on a fixed window so a hostile burst from one tenant does not poison the shared envelope shape.

When the cap is hit the offending dimension is dropped silently (no 4xx); a dedicated metric tracks the drop for ops visibility. See `docs/A30.md` (decision log) for the rationale on why this path drops rather than rejects.

max_per_window.description: Maximum admissions per window. Exceeding this floors further admissions in the same window to drop-with-metric.

window_seconds.description: Window length in seconds. Counters rotate per `floor(now / window_seconds)` slot.

capture.description: Master capture switch. When `false`, [`capture_user_id`] always returns `None`.

max_length.description: Maximum allowed length of the resolved user ID. Values longer than this are dropped wholesale (no truncation, since truncation collides distinct identities).

budget.description: Cap on captured user IDs per workspace per window. `None` disables the gate (default). When the cap is hit, the dimension is dropped silently and `sbproxy_capture_budget_dropped_total{workspace,dimension="user"}` is incremented.

RawForwardRule.description:
One forward rule on an origin: a set of matcher entries plus the inline child origin to dispatch to when any entry hits.

Compiled at config-load time. The runtime walks the `rules` of each forward rule against the incoming request and uses the first matching entry's `origin`. Within a single entry the present matchers (path, header, query) are ANDed; across entries they are ORed.

rules.description: Path matchers. The rule fires when any one of these matches the request path.

origin.description: Inline child origin executed when the rule fires.

parameters.description:
Parameter declarations that apply to every matcher in this rule.

Mirrors the OpenAPI 3.0 Parameter Object verbatim so emission is a near-direct map. Used by OpenAPI emission to populate `paths.<path>.<method>.parameters[]` and is exposed on the request context as `path_params` after the matcher captures values.

ForwardRuleMatcher.description:
One match entry inside a forward rule's `rules:` list.

Each entry may carry any combination of `path`, `header`, and `query` matchers. Within a single entry the matchers are ANDed: every present matcher must succeed for the entry to fire. Across entries in the same rule the semantics are OR: any matching entry triggers the rule. The shorthand `match: <prefix>` is equivalent to `path: { prefix: ... }`.

match.description: Shorthand for a prefix match. Equivalent to `path: { prefix: <value> }`.

PathMatcher.description:
A path matcher inside a forward rule.

Exactly one of `prefix`, `exact`, `template`, or `regex` should be set. Precedence when more than one is provided: `template` > `regex` > `exact` > `prefix`. Templates and regex are evaluated lazily, so origins that only use prefix/exact pay no regex cost.

Template syntax (`/users/{id}/posts/{post_id}`) supports named segments, catch-all (`/static/{*rest}`), and optional per-segment regex constraints (`/users/{id:[0-9]+}`). Constraint compilation happens at config-load time; the runtime only re-validates constrained params after the trie match.

prefix.description: Matches any path that starts with this prefix.

exact.description: Matches only this exact path string.

template.description: OpenAPI-style path template with named segments. Captured params are exposed on the request context as `path_params` for downstream modifiers, CEL/Lua scripts, and metrics labels.

regex.description: Whole-path regex escape hatch. Use named captures (`(?P<id>...)`) to surface params on the request context.

HeaderMatcher.description:
Match a request header by exact value or value prefix.

Exactly one of `value` or `prefix` should be set. When both are present `value` wins (exact comparison). Header name matching is case-insensitive per RFC 7230; value comparison is case-sensitive.

prefix.description: Required value prefix. Ignored when `value` is set.

QueryMatcher.description:
Match a query string parameter by exact value.

The query string is parsed as `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`. The matcher succeeds if any occurrence of `name` equals `value`. When `value` is omitted the matcher succeeds whenever the parameter is present at all.

value.description: Required exact value. When `None`, presence of the parameter is enough.

ForwardRuleOrigin.description: Inline child origin used when a forward rule fires. Carries the action plus optional request modifiers and identifying metadata.

id.description: Optional identifier used in metrics and logs.

hostname.description: Optional hostname tag (informational; the parent origin's hostname is what routed the request).

action.description: Action executed when the rule fires. Stays as raw JSON because action types are plugin-extensible (registered via the inventory crate).

request_modifiers.description: Optional request modifiers applied before the action runs.

Parameter.description:
An OpenAPI 3.0 Parameter Object declared on a forward rule.

Field names and shapes mirror the OpenAPI spec exactly so emission is a direct passthrough. The `schema` field is kept as `serde_json::Value` because the OpenAPI Schema Object is large and we forward it verbatim.

name.description: Parameter name. For path params this must match a `{name}` segment in one of the rule's `template` matchers.

required.description: Whether the parameter is required. Path params are always required per the OpenAPI spec; emission enforces this even when `false`.

description.description: Free-form description surfaced in the emitted spec.

schema.description: OpenAPI Schema Object (e.g. `{ "type": "integer", "format": "int64" }`). Forwarded verbatim into the emitted spec.

ParameterLocation.description:
Location of an OpenAPI parameter (`in:` field).

Matches the OpenAPI 3.0 enum exactly. `cookie` is intentionally not supported here yet because the gateway has no per-cookie capture story.

oneOf.description: A captured `{name}` segment from a `template` matcher.
A query string parameter.
A request header.

ErrorPageEntry.description: One per-status custom error page entry. Multiple entries for the same status code are content-negotiated against the inbound request's `Accept` header.

status.description: Which HTTP status code(s) this entry covers.

body.description: Response body. When `template = true`, the proxy substitutes `{{ status_code }}` and `{{ request.path }}` (with or without surrounding whitespace) at request time.

template.description: When true, treat `body` as a template and run substitution.

StatusSpec.description: Status code spec for an [`ErrorPageEntry`]. Either a single integer (`status: 401`) or a list (`status: [401, 403]`). The list form is the historical authored shape; the single-int form is a sugar.

anyOf.description: Multiple status codes; any match counts.

ProblemDetailsConfig.description:
RFC 9457 Problem Details default-renderer configuration.

When enabled, any proxy-generated error response that is *not* already matched by a custom [`ErrorPageEntry`] is rendered as `application/problem+json` per RFC 9457. The two configs compose: operators can author per-status custom pages and still opt in to problem-details as a structured fallback for everything else.

Spec: <https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html>.

enabled.description: Whether to render unmatched proxy-generated errors as `application/problem+json`. Defaults to `false`; existing operators see no behavior change unless they opt in.

type_base_uri.description: Base URI for the `type` field. When set to e.g. `https://api.example.com/errors`, status 503 renders as `type: https://api.example.com/errors/503`. When unset, the renderer emits the RFC 9457 default `about:blank`.

include_detail.description: When true (the default), the renderer copies the proxy's internal error message into the `detail` field. Operators who route problem responses to external clients can set this to false to avoid leaking upstream error text.

ProxyStatusConfig.description:
RFC 9209 `Proxy-Status` response header configuration.

When `enabled`, the proxy stamps a structured `Proxy-Status` header on every non-2xx response. The header carries the proxy identity, the upstream status, and an optional `error` parameter derived from the upstream failure mode. Operators consuming the header can diagnose forwarding errors without scraping the body.

Spec: <https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9209.html>.

enabled.description: Whether to stamp the `Proxy-Status` header on non-2xx responses. Defaults to `false`; opt in per origin so existing operator dashboards that match on bare status codes are not surprised.

identity.description: Proxy identity token used as the first parameter of the header (per RFC 9209's grammar). Defaults to `sbproxy`. Operators running a fleet can override this for branding (e.g. `acme-edge`, `sbproxy-eu-west-1`).

MirrorConfig.description:
Per-origin shadow-traffic configuration.

When set on an origin, the proxy fires a fire-and-forget copy of each request at `url` and discards the response. The primary upstream is never blocked by mirror delivery. Useful for safe rollouts of new backends and replay-driven testing.

url.description: Mirror upstream URL (http:// or https://). IPv6 hosts must be bracketed in the URL (e.g. `http://[2001:db8::1]:8080`) per RFC 3986.

sample_rate.description: Sample rate in `[0.0, 1.0]`. `1.0` (default) mirrors every request; `0.1` mirrors ~10%. Sampling is per-request and uses a uniform PRNG; do not rely on exact counts.

timeout_ms.description: Mirror request timeout in milliseconds. Independent from the primary upstream timeout. Default 5000ms.

mirror_body.description: Whether to tee the inbound request body into the mirror request. Default `false`: the mirror sees only method, path, query, and headers (sufficient for read endpoints, GET-mostly traffic, and any case where shadow-replaying writes is unsafe). Set to `true` to enable body teeing for shadow-replay of POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints during migrations.

max_body_bytes.description: Maximum bytes of body to mirror. Bodies larger than this cap are skipped (the mirror is fired without a body) so a single large upload cannot blow up proxy memory. Default `1048576` (1 MiB).

MessageSignaturesConfig.description:
RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures verification configuration.

When `verify: true`, the proxy enforces RFC 9421 signature verification on every inbound request to this origin. Requests without a valid `Signature-Input` + `Signature` header pair matching the configured `key_id` are rejected with `401 Unauthorized` and `WWW-Authenticate: Signature` before any downstream auth provider runs.

`algorithm` is `hmac_sha256` or `ed25519`. `key` carries the shared secret (HMAC) or the base64/hex-encoded raw 32-byte public key (Ed25519). `required_components` is the optional set of canonical components every accepted signature must cover. `clock_skew_seconds` defaults to 30s.

Spec: <https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9421.html>.

verify.description: Whether to enforce signature verification on inbound requests.

key_id.description: The `keyid` value the signer is expected to advertise.

required_components.description: Optional canonical components every accepted signature must cover.

clock_skew_seconds.description: Optional clock skew tolerance in seconds. Defaults to 30s.

OlpConfig.description: `/introspect` (RFC 7662) is deferred to a follow-up PR because it requires a revocation / nonce store.

enabled.description: Master toggle. When false the well-known endpoints 404.

signing_key.description: Ed25519 signing key, hex-encoded 32-byte seed. Operators generate one with `openssl rand -hex 32` or read it from a secret store. The secret-resolver pass honours provider-specific references at config-load time, mirroring how other key-material fields work.

key_id.description: `kid` the JWS header advertises. Rotation appends a new key with a new kid and trusts both for the cutover window.

issuer.description: Issuer URL stamped onto issued tokens (typically the proxy's public base URL such as `https://api.example.com`).

default_scope.description: Default scope token list (space-separated, per RFC 8693 §2.2.1). Mints with `scope_override == None` use this value.

default_ttl_secs.description: Default TTL applied to issued tokens (seconds). Mints with `ttl_secs_override == None` use this value.

content_key_seed.description: WOR-808 PR8: optional Encrypted Media Standard content-key seed (hex-encoded). When set, every issued OLP token carries an RFC 7800 `cnf.jwk` claim with a per-token AES-256-GCM key derived via HKDF(seed, salt=jti, info="ems-content-key"). Decryptors that retain the jti can recompute the key without storing the material. Absent leaves the cnf claim off the token so EMS-unaware clients keep working.

introspect.description: WOR-808 PR9: introspect / revoke surface. Absent leaves both `/.well-known/olp/introspect` (RFC 7662) and `/.well-known/olp/revoke` (RFC 7009) 404'd. Set to enable.

OlpIntrospectConfig.description:
WOR-808 PR9: RFC 7662 OAuth Token Introspection + RFC 7009 Token Revocation configuration for the OLP issuer.

When this block is present the proxy exposes:

* `POST /.well-known/olp/introspect` — RFC 7662 §2 introspection. Returns `{ "active": true, ... }` for valid + un-revoked tokens issued by this origin's signing key, mirroring every OLP claim. Returns `{ "active": false }` for any token that does not verify, has expired, or has been revoked (§2.2 forbids leaking the reason). * `POST /.well-known/olp/revoke` — RFC 7009 §2. Writes the token's `jti` to the configured revocation store with a TTL that matches the token's remaining lifetime, so subsequent introspections return `active: false`.

Both endpoints share one `auth` policy because the same actor that can ask "is this token active" should also be able to assert "this token is no longer trusted." Rate-limiting on `active: false` responses (RFC 7662 §2.1 scan-attack defence) and DPoP-bound confirmation checks ship in a follow-up PR.

enabled.description: Master toggle. When false the well-known endpoints 404 even if the rest of the block is configured. Lets an operator wire the auth + store ahead of time and flip it on later.

introspect_path.description: Path the introspection endpoint binds to. Defaults to `/.well-known/olp/introspect` so the OLP cluster of endpoints stays under one prefix.

revoke_path.description: Path the revocation endpoint binds to. Defaults to `/.well-known/olp/revoke`.

auth.description: Caller auth policy. Required for both endpoints; RFC 7662 §2.1 MUSTs "some form of authorization" to prevent token-scanning. Defaults to `mode: self` which uses the token-being-introspected as its own proof of possession (works without any operator configuration).

realm.description: `Basic` realm advertised on 401 challenges. Defaults to `"olp-introspect"`; operators with multi-tenant deployments often want one realm per tenant for log clarity.

revocation_store.description: Revocation-store backend. Without a store, `/revoke` 503s and `/introspect` reports `active: true` for every otherwise-valid token (RFC 7662 §2.2's "active" is only signature + exp). The `memory` default is sufficient for a single-process dev box but does NOT survive restart; production deployments should pick `redb` or `redis`.

mirror_cnf.description: Whether to mirror the token's optional `cnf` (RFC 7800) confirmation claim onto the introspect response. Defaults to true so EMS-bound tokens carry their content key through to the relying party in one round trip; operators concerned about disclosing the key over a shared introspect connection can flip to false to require the RP to fetch the JWS directly.

OlpIntrospectAuth.description:
Auth policy for the introspect + revoke endpoints. Three modes:

* `self` (default) — the caller proves possession of the token by sending the same value in `Authorization: License <token>` *and* in the `token=` form parameter. Reasonable for the common "RP introspects tokens it already holds" case and requires no operator credential management. * `basic` — HTTP Basic with operator-managed credentials. Pass `{ username, password_hash }` pairs in `clients`; passwords are stored as Argon2id hashes. RFC 7662 §2.1's "client authentication" path. * `none` — no auth. ONLY appropriate for fully-private deployments behind a service mesh that already authenticates the caller. The proxy logs a `warn!` at startup when this is selected.

oneOf.description: Caller proves possession of the token they are introspecting.
HTTP Basic with operator-managed credentials.

clients.description: One entry per authorized caller. Empty list rejects every request with 401 so an operator cannot accidentally deploy `mode: basic` without setting up any credentials.

oneOf.description: No auth (private-deployment escape hatch).

password_hash.description: Argon2id hash of the password (PHC string format, as produced by `argon2 -t 3 -m 65536 -p 4 -i`). The proxy verifies with the same parameters; supports secret references via the secret-resolver pass.

oneOf.description: Process-local, lost on restart. Default; appropriate for dev and CI only.
On-disk redb file. Single-process; survives restart, ACID. Production default for single-replica deployments.

path.description: Filesystem path to the redb file. The path is created on first use; the operator MUST ensure the directory is writable by the proxy user.

oneOf.description: Redis (shared across replicas). Use for horizontally-scaled deployments where a token revoked on one replica must be observed by all the others.

url.description: `redis://` connection URL. Pool size and timeouts inherit the workspace `redis` defaults.

WebBotAuthPublishConfig.description:
WOR-808 PR7: Open License Protocol (OLP) issuer configuration.

When set, the proxy stands up two well-known endpoints on the origin:

- `POST /.well-known/olp/token` — issues a license token signed with the configured Ed25519 key, body shaped per RFC 6749 (`access_token` + `token_type: "License"` + `expires_in`). - `GET /.well-known/olp/key` — publishes the verification JWK set (RFC 7517) so external introspectors can verify tokens without contacting the issuer per-token.

WOR-805 AC#4: Web Bot Auth publish config. When enabled the proxy serves two unauthenticated well-known endpoints on this origin:

* `GET /.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory` — JWKS document carrying SBproxy's own Ed25519 signing-key public key. Verifiers (Cloudflare, AWS WAF, any third-party origin that runs a Web Bot Auth verifier) fetch this to verify the `Signature-Input` + `Signature` headers SBproxy attaches to outbound requests. * `GET /.well-known/web-bot-auth/agent-card` — the discovery document that points verifiers at the directory; carries the operator-facing agent name, description, and contact URL.

`public_key_hex` is the 32-byte Ed25519 public key, hex-encoded (the SECRET side never crosses this config; the signer that runs upstream of the proxy holds it). `key_id` is the `kid` the signer advertises and the directory JWK publishes.

Operators who do not configure this block expose neither endpoint; requests to those paths fall through to the upstream proxy (or return 404 if no route matches).

enabled.description: Whether the publish endpoints are enabled.

key_id.description: `kid` the JWK + Signature Agent Card advertise. Stable across rotations of unrelated keys so an old `keyid=` reference in a signed request still resolves.

public_key_hex.description: Ed25519 public key, hex-encoded (32 bytes → 64 hex chars).

agent_name.description: Operator-facing agent name on the Signature Agent Card.

directory_url.description: `directory_url` the agent card points at. Must be `https://`; every Web Bot Auth verifier rejects plaintext.

description.description: Optional description shown alongside the agent name.

contact_url.description: Optional contact URL (mailto:, https://, etc.) for misuse reports.

signing_key_hex.description: Optional 32-byte Ed25519 private seed, hex-encoded (64 hex chars). When set, the directory and agent-card HTTP responses are self-signed per RFC 9421 over `("content-digest")` with `tag="web-bot-auth"` and `keyid=key_id`. Verifiers can then confirm the body they fetched was emitted by the holder of the key the directory advertises, closing the trust loop without relying solely on TLS. Absent leaves the responses unsigned; the Web Bot Auth IETF draft permits both shapes and verifiers MAY treat unsigned directories as lower-trust. The secret-resolver pass honours secret references at config load so the raw seed never has to live in the YAML.

IdempotencyConfig.description:
RFC 8594-style idempotency middleware configuration.

When `enabled`, the proxy reads an idempotency key from the configured request header (default: `Idempotency-Key`), hashes the request body for conflict detection, and serves cached responses on retries. Workspace-isolated keys mean two workspaces using the same key never collide. The middleware engages only on the listed HTTP methods; defaults to POST, PUT, and PATCH.

Backed by `sbproxy_middleware::idempotency` (the cache backend trait + memory / Redis impls). For Redis-backed clusters set `backend: redis`; the cache binds to the cluster L2 store at compile time. Single-instance deployments leave `backend: memory` (the default).

enabled.description: Whether to engage the idempotency middleware on this origin. Defaults to false; opt in per origin.

header_name.description: Request header name carrying the idempotency key. Defaults to `Idempotency-Key`.

ttl_secs.description: Time-to-live for cached entries, in seconds. Defaults to 86400 (24 hours).

methods.description: HTTP methods the middleware engages on. Defaults to `[POST, PUT, PATCH]`. Other methods (idempotent by HTTP spec) pass through unaffected.

backend.description: Cache backend selector. `memory` (the default) uses an in-process LRU; `redis` binds to the cluster L2 store at compile time. Operators who set `redis` without configuring `proxy.l2_store` get an error at config-validate time.

max_request_body_bytes.description: Maximum request body size in bytes that the middleware will buffer for the cache check. Requests larger than this cap gracefully degrade: the middleware skips caching for that request and stamps `x-sbproxy-idempotency: SKIPPED-OVERSIZE-REQUEST` on the response so operators can see the skip. Defaults to 1 MiB.

max_response_body_bytes.description: Maximum response body size in bytes that will be buffered for caching. Responses larger than this cap stream to the client uncached; the next retry with the same key falls through to the upstream. Defaults to 1 MiB.

max_concurrent_buffers.description: Process-wide cap on the number of concurrent buffered idempotency requests *for this origin*. When the pool is exhausted, new requests skip caching and stream normally; `x-sbproxy-idempotency: SKIPPED-POOL-FULL` is stamped so operators can spot pool pressure. Defaults to 256, which at the default per-request cap gives a 256 MiB worst-case memory budget per origin.

oneOf.description: In-process cache. Default. Suitable for single-instance deployments and per-replica idempotency in clusters where retries land on the same replica.
Cluster-wide cache backed by the shared L2 store (`proxy.l2_store`). Required for clusters where retries may land on different replicas.

ConnectionPoolConfig.description:
Per-origin connection pool tuning parameters.

Controls how many concurrent connections are maintained to an upstream, how long idle connections are kept alive, and the maximum lifetime of any individual connection.

max_connections.description:
Maximum number of concurrent connections to the upstream.

Additional requests will queue until a connection is available. Default: 128.

idle_timeout_secs.description:
Maximum idle time before a connection is closed, in seconds.

Connections that have been unused for longer than this will be dropped from the pool.  Default: 90 s.

max_lifetime_secs.description:
Maximum total lifetime of a connection, in seconds.

Connections older than this will be closed and replaced even if they are still healthy.  Default: 300 s.

AgentSkillEntry.description:
One entry in an origin's `agent_skills:` advertisement.

The shape mirrors the v0.2.0 manifest entry described at `https://schemas.agentskills.io/discovery/0.2.0/schema.json`: every entry carries a stable name, a kind discriminator, a human description, and the URL the agent fetches to retrieve the artifact. A `digest` field is computed at config-load time by hashing the resolved artifact bytes; the per-request handler re-hashes the body on every serve and refuses to ship a tampered artifact.

The optional safety knobs (`max_decompression_ratio`, `max_entries`, `max_expanded_bytes`, `max_clock_skew_secs`) cap archive parsing so a malicious origin cannot zip-bomb a downstream agent. All four have sensible defaults, and v1 configs that omit `agent_skills:` pay nothing for the new schema field.

name.description: Stable identifier (used as the manifest `name` and as the audit-event subject). Must be unique within the origin's `agent_skills:` list.

type.description: Discriminator: `skill-md` for a single Markdown body or `archive` for a `.tar.gz` / `.zip` bundle. The v0.2.0 spec reserves these two values; any other input is rejected at config-load time.

description.description: Human-readable description surfaced in the manifest. Reads like a one-line capability summary.

url.description:
URL the agent fetches to retrieve the artifact. May be:

- A path-absolute reference (`/skills/foo.md`) re-hosted by the proxy on the same origin. - A fully-qualified URL fetched once at config-load and re-emitted verbatim in the manifest (the proxy does not re-host external artifacts). - A relative reference (`skills/foo.md`) resolved per RFC 3986 against the request authority at serve time.

visibility.description: Visibility gate. `public` (default) returns the entry to every caller. `authenticated` filters the entry out of the manifest served to anonymous callers; the proxy still recomputes the digest per request so caching does not leak filtered entries.

path.description: Local filesystem path to the artifact body, when the operator hosts the file alongside the config. Used for `skill-md` entries with a path-absolute or relative `url`. When neither `path` nor `body` is set and `url` is path-absolute, the compiler resolves the path relative to the workspace root.

body.description: Inline literal body. Useful for short skill files without having to commit a separate Markdown file. Mutually exclusive with `path`; when both are set the compiler prefers `path`.

max_decompression_ratio.description: Maximum decompression ratio (compressed:expanded) tolerated for `archive` entries. Default 100. Refuses to extract archives whose total expanded size exceeds the cap.

max_entries.description: Maximum entry count per archive. Default 1000.

max_expanded_bytes.description: Maximum expanded byte budget per archive. Default 10 MiB.

max_clock_skew_secs.description: Per-entry clock-skew tolerance in seconds for any time-sensitive header attached to the artifact response. Default 60. Reserved: the v0.2.0 ship attaches no such header today; the field exists so a follow-up that signs each artifact body can wire its own freshness check without a config-schema break.

AgentsJsonConfig.description: Per-origin agents.json manifest configuration (WOR-820). See the [`RawOriginConfig::agents_json`] field and the agents.json v0.1 spec at <https://github.com/wild-card-ai/agents-json>.

sources.description: API sources. When omitted, the proxy emits a single source pointing at this origin's `/.well-known/openapi.json`. Each entry must carry an `id` and a `path` per the spec.

flows.description: Operator-authored flow objects, emitted verbatim. Each flow must be schema-valid (`id`, `title`, `description`, `actions`, `fields`); the proxy does not synthesize flows.

overrides.description: Optional `overrides` array, emitted verbatim when present.

AgentsJsonInfo.description: The `info` block of an agents.json manifest.

OriginObservabilityConfig.description: Origin-scope observability sub-tree. The `log.redact` block, `log.sinks` (origin-scoped fan-out, filtered by the stamped `route`), and `log.custom_fields` (the most-specific access-log fields, which override tenant- and proxy-scope fields of the same name) are all consumed at runtime.

OriginObservabilityLogConfig.description: Origin-scope `log:` sub-block. Mirrors the proxy-scope and tenant-scope shape; exposes redaction plus origin-scoped sinks (WOR-1045 PR2). The dispatcher routes every record whose stamped `route` matches this origin's hostname into each declared sink.

sinks.description: Origin-scoped log sinks. Each sink's `name` is unique within this origin; cross-scope collisions with a tenant or proxy `sinks:` entry are intentional (they are different sinks). Sinks at this scope default to the `external` redaction profile.

custom_fields.description: Origin-scoped custom access-log fields. Same shape as the proxy-scope `custom_fields:`. A field defined here is the most specific: it overrides a tenant- or proxy-scope field with the same `name` for requests routed to this origin.

OriginObservabilityRedactConfig.description: Origin-scope `redact:` sub-block. Carries the per-origin overrides for the field-key denylist (WOR-1042 `fields:`, additive), the operator regex pass (WOR-1042 `patterns:` + `disable:`), and the rule-driven PII redactor (WOR-1043 `pii:`).

fields.description: WOR-1042: origin-scope additions to the field-key denylist. Additive only on top of the merged proxy + tenant set; an origin cannot disable a parent denylist entry.

patterns.description: WOR-1042: origin-scope additions to the regex pattern set. Additive on top of proxy + tenant. Use `disable:` to opt out of a more-general pattern by name.

disable.description: WOR-1042: pattern names to opt out of at this origin. Resolved against the merged proxy + tenant pattern set; the built-in field-key denylist is never disable-able.

pii.description: Origin-scope override for the tenant-scope (or proxy-scope when the origin has no tenant) PII pass. Resolution rules: the origin inherits the parent scope's `enabled` flag and rule set, then ADDS its own `rules:` entries and SUBTRACTS its own `disable:` entries. An explicit `enabled: false` opts the origin out even when parent scopes enable PII. See [`ObservabilityPiiConfig`].

AccessLogConfig.description:
Structured-JSON access-log emission, off by default.

When `enabled` is true, every completed request emits one JSON line via the tracing `access_log` target after status, method, and sampling filters are applied. The actual record shape is `AccessLogEntry` in `sbproxy-observe`; this struct only governs whether and which records are emitted.

Filter semantics: - `status_codes` empty matches every status; non-empty restricts to the listed codes. - `methods` empty matches every method; non-empty restricts to the listed methods (case-insensitive on emit). - `sample_rate` is applied last and accepts a value in `[0.0, 1.0]`.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false (the default), no access-log lines are emitted regardless of the other fields.

sample_rate.description: Probability in `[0.0, 1.0]` that a matching request is logged. Defaults to `1.0` (log every match).

status_codes.description: HTTP status codes to log. Empty (the default) means log all statuses.

methods.description: HTTP methods to log. Empty (the default) means log all methods. Comparison is case-insensitive.

capture_headers.description: Header capture configuration. Off by default; opt in by listing header names (or `*`) in `request` / `response`. Captured values land in the `request_headers` / `response_headers` fields of the emitted entry.

slow_request_threshold_ms.description: Log every request at or above this latency, regardless of `sample_rate`. `None` preserves sampler-only behaviour.

output.description: Output sink. Defaults to stderr/tracing target.

CaptureHeadersConfig.description:
Allowlist-driven header capture for the access log.

Lists are matched after lowercasing both the configured names and the inbound header names. Two pattern shapes are accepted:

* Exact name (`"user-agent"`, `"x-cache"`). * `"*"` to capture every header (subject to the sensitive-header denylist below). * Trailing-glob (`"x-ratelimit-*"`) to capture every header whose name starts with the prefix before the `*`. Only one trailing `*` is supported; embedded wildcards are treated as literal.

A hardcoded denylist of sensitive headers (`authorization`, `cookie`, `set-cookie`, `proxy-authorization`, `x-api-key`) is excluded from `*` and glob matches. To capture one of these, list it by exact name; the proxy logs a `WARN` at config load so the choice is visible.

request.description: Request-side allowlist. Empty (the default) captures nothing.

response.description: Response-side allowlist. Empty (the default) captures nothing.

max_value_bytes.description: Per-value byte cap. Captured header values longer than this are truncated to the cap with a trailing `"..."` (the suffix counts toward the cap). Defaults to 1024.

redact_pii.description: Run the `sbproxy-security` PII redactor over captured header values. Off by default. Independent from the cheap secret-key scan that already runs over every emitted access-log line.

redact_pii_rules.description:
Optional rule-name filter for `redact_pii`. Empty (the default) uses the full built-in rule set; non-empty restricts to the listed rule names (`email`, `us_ssn`, `credit_card`, `phone_us`, `ipv4`, `openai_key`, `anthropic_key`, `aws_access`, `github_token`).

The same rule list is shared by [`Self::redact_pii_other_fields`] when that knob is on, so operators only configure one rule list for both the header scope and the non-header scope.

redact_pii_other_fields.description: Run the same `sbproxy-security` PII redactor over the non-header access-log fields that can carry PII: `path`, `user_id`, `properties` values (keys are left untouched), and `model`. Off by default. Independent of [`Self::redact_pii`] so operators can adopt header redaction first and the broader scope later (or either alone). Reuses [`Self::redact_pii_rules`] for the rule filter; the cheap `redact_secrets` pass over the full JSON line still runs regardless of this knob.

max_size_mb.description: Rotate before writing when the file is at least this size.

max_backups.description: Number of rotated backups to retain.

AgentClassesConfig.description:
Top-level `agent_classes:` block. Tunes the agent-class resolver the binary constructs at startup and threads through the request pipeline.

The block is fully optional: when absent the binary builds the resolver from `AgentClassCatalog::defaults()` plus the default resolver tuning. Most operators leave it untouched.

catalog.description: Catalog source. `builtin` (default) loads the embedded YAML catalog. `inline` loads the entries in `entries`. `hosted-feed` fetches from `hosted_feed.url`. `merged` loads the hosted feed and overlays it on top of the embedded defaults so an operator's feed only needs to ship deltas.

entries.description: Inline catalog entries. Used when `catalog: inline`; each entry is validated by the runtime against the same schema as the embedded catalog.

hosted_feed.description: Hosted-feed configuration. Required when `catalog: hosted-feed` or `catalog: merged`.

resolver.description: Resolver tuning (rDNS toggle, bot-auth toggle, cache size). Each field has a sensible default; this block is rarely needed.

HostedFeedConfig.description:
Hosted-feed source for the agent-class catalog.

Pulled at startup and refreshed on a schedule the registry owns. The fetch loop is not implemented in this crate; the field is reserved here so YAML written against the merged or hosted-feed shapes parses cleanly.

url.description: Feed URL. Plain `http://` is allowed only against `127.0.0.1` and `localhost` for local development; the registry crate enforces HTTPS at fetch time for any other host.

bootstrap_keys.description: Bootstrap public keys (base64-encoded ed25519 keys) used to verify the feed's detached signature on the first fetch. Empty in dev configs; required for production.

AgentClassResolverConfig.description:
Resolver-tuning knobs for the agent-class chain.

All fields have sensible defaults: rDNS verification on, bot-auth keyid lookup on, 10 000-entry verdict cache. Operators set fields only when they need to disable a specific signal (typically rDNS in environments without a working PTR resolver).

rdns_enabled.description: Run forward-confirmed reverse-DNS as resolver step 2. Default `true`. Disable when the runtime has no working DNS resolver.

bot_auth_keyid_enabled.description: Honour the verified Web Bot Auth `keyid` as resolver step 1. Default `true`. Off forces the resolver to fall through to rDNS / UA matching even when bot-auth verified the request.

cache_size.description: Per-process verdict cache capacity (rDNS verdicts only). 10 000 is the default; bump for very high-cardinality IP populations.

RateLimitsConfig.description: WOR-1130: top-level workspace rate-limit budget configuration.

workspace_default.description: Budget applied to the default workspace (the only workspace in the OSS single-tenant build; enterprise multi-tenant resolves a per-tenant budget).

clock.description: Clock source for the token-bucket refill + suspend cool-down. `system` (default) uses wall time; `manual` advances only via the `/api/rate_limits/clock/advance` admin endpoint (tests).

http_rps_sustained.description: Sustained inbound HTTP requests-per-second ceiling (the token bucket refill rate).

http_rps_burst.description: Burst ceiling (the token bucket capacity). Requests above this within one window are throttled.

soft_threshold_rps.description: Soft observation threshold. Traffic above this but below the sustained ceiling emits `sbproxy_rate_limit_total{result="soft"}` without throttling, so operators see the climb early.

abuse_threshold_throttle_to_suspend.description: Consecutive-throttle count that promotes a workspace from `Throttle` to `AutoSuspend`. A2.5 default is 1000.

auto_suspend_cooldown_secs.description: Cool-down (seconds) a workspace stays auto-suspended before it drops back to `Throttle`. A2.5 default is 3600.

RateLimitClockMode.description: WOR-1130: clock source for the rate-limit budget.

oneOf.description: Wall-clock time (production default).
Test clock advanced only via the admin endpoint.

sink.description: Where admin-action audit rows are kept.

oneOf.description: Keep the last N rows in memory, queryable via `/api/audit/recent`.
Emit to the structured `security_audit` tracing target only.

enabled.description: Turn ledger emission on. When false (the default), the `tools/call` path pays a single atomic load and emits nothing.

path.description: NDJSON output path for the `file` sink. Required when `sink: file`; ignored otherwise.

oneOf.description: Emit each record as a structured `session_ledger` tracing line.
Append each record as one NDJSON line to `path`.

UpdateConfig.description:
The `update:` block: how `sbproxy update` behaves (WOR-1804).

Pinning always wins. A `path` / `brew` / `apt`-managed artifact, or one pinned to an explicit version or digest, is reported but never replaced unless a run explicitly targets it. A background check (see `auto`) only ever reports; applying an update is always an explicit `sbproxy update` run.

channel.description: Release stream the binary and managed engines follow.

auto.description: When true, a background freshness check runs every `check_interval` and reports to `sbproxy doctor` and the logs. A background check never replaces an artifact; it only reports.

check_interval_secs.description: How often the background check runs, in seconds. Accepts a humanized duration (`6h`, `1d`) or bare seconds. Only consulted when `auto` is true. Defaults to once a day.

UpdateChannel.description: Which release stream `sbproxy update` follows for the binary and the managed inference engines (WOR-1804).

oneOf.description: Track the latest stable release. The default.
Track the newest release, pre-releases included.
Never move automatically. Every artifact is held; only an `sbproxy update` run that explicitly targets an artifact may replace it.
